physics iit jodhpur
welcome to the department of physics the subject of physics is inherently natural science subject based on strong theoretical foundations and experimental findings
the subject closely intersects with almost all the branches of engineering and the current research activities of physics across the world made huge impact on new advanced and emerging technologies
keeping these facts into consideration our department is committed to imparting high quality education based on strong academic curriculum comprising elements of theoretical concepts laboratory experiments and most importantly hands on experiments
the graduates of the department are expected to have deep insight into the domain of physics together with strong bent of mind to pursue cutting edge research and translate it into technology
we offer broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs
our year bs in physics with specialization is technology oriented science program and offers specialization in physics based emerging technology domains
the program also has the flexibility to convert into year bs tech
dual degree course
at the postgraduate level we have four degree programs viz
years sc tech dual degree in physics and materials engineering
the dual degree program also has additional two specializations namely functional materials and computational materials engineering
finally our ph program empowers scholars to pursue research in various domains
research areas the department has very good blend of highly qualified young and experienced faculty members in the areas of advanced physics
the department is also equipped with number of thematic laboratories which enable the students to carry out high end and impactful theoretical and applied research in the following focus areas research areas high energy physics and astrophysics theoretical research in domains flavour physics astrophysics particle physics cosmology and dark matter and dark energy
experimental research in flavor physics with the belle ii experiment
quantum physics open quantum systems theoretical and experimental aspects of quantum optics quantum information quantum field theory quantum statistical mechanics and quantum cosmology
condensed matter and plasma physics group experimental and theoretical research on molecular electronics organic electronics cold plasma technologies and applications energy storage solar cells light emitting diodes solar water splitting soft matters statistical physics computational physics multiscale modelling phase ordering dynamics glasses amorphous solids polymers and solid electrolyte materials physics of living matter active matter physics functional materials materials design and engineering nanomagnetism and multiferroics
optics and photonics theoretical and experimental research in nano photonics light in disordered media ultrafast optics nonlinear optics topological singularities specially optical fibers photonic sensors time dynamic systems terahertz generation and application transient and steady state spectroscopy bound state in the continuum disorder photons and light matter interaction and ceramic materials for high energy laser applications
candidates interested in pursuing ph please go through the research groups and visit the individual faculty home pages
the application can be submitted with this link
infrastructure the department is equipped with high end research equipment which includes squid magnetometer physical property measurement systems ppms raman spectrometer and scanning tunnelling microscope stm besides host of other advanced characterization equipment which is available at the centre for research development of scientific instruments crdsi
collaborations the department is having close collaboration with number of national and international agencies including drdo isro dae csir dst and serb
department faculties also have collaborations with various institutes of national and international repute namely tifr iit bombay iit gandhinagar iit bhu central university of rajasthan university of montreal canada university of warsaw poland brookhaven national laboratory usa eth zurich switzerland imem parma italy infn turino italy cmu pittsburgh usa belle ii kek japan etc
our social media connect contact numbers for new students for any queries regarding registration accessing facilities etc phone numbers office of physics highlights view all highlights prof ram prakash from the department of physics iit jodhpur has been elected as the president of the plasma science society of india mr shubham chamola has received the prestigious commonwealth fellowship to work at the university of cambridge uk for duration of one year nov oct dr yogeshwar nath mishra has co invented the world fastest single shot wide field microscope with his collaborators at nasa caltech in usa and fau in germany
they published their work in the journ jitendra yadav has received the galileo galilei poster award at the international symposium on beyond li ion batteries held in padova italy sept
ph students sipra mohapatra and hema teherpuria have been awarded the prestigious aps travel grant and best poster award for hema at ccp2024 our phd student miss surbhi ramawat has received the best poster award with cash prize of euro in one of the globally renowned conference on thermoelectric materials

chemistry iit jodhpur
welcome to department of chemistry head message warm welcome to the department of chemistry at iit jodhpur which was established in
since the inception the department has taken major strides forward in teaching research and technology development
our department offers variety of academic programs including sc
materials engineering with specialization bs in chemistry with specialization years and ph programs
we also provide core and elective courses in chemistry and related fields for undergraduate tech
the department offers chemistry courses for year integrated sc ed
program itep which is in alignment with nep to support teacher preparation and foster professional development
besides teaching the department is striving to be acknowledged for excellence in research and technology
our goal is to foster an environment where everyone can thrive reach their full potential and contribute to cutting edge scientific advancements
we have the latest state of the art basic teaching laboratories and several thematic research laboratories
our current faculty strength is and they are carrying out research activities in all major areas of chemical sciences namely organic synthesis and catalysis chemical biology main group chemistry and organometallics nanomaterials spectroscopy and chemical dynamics biophysical chemistry and computational chemistry
the cutting edge research in the thrust areas by our faculty members are published in international peer reviewed journals of well repute
in short iit jodhpur chemistry department is where chemistry meets technology through collaboration communication and cooperation
encourage the future aspirants to get enrolled at iit jodhpur and wish you all the very best in your future endeavours

bioscience bioengineering iit jodhpur
welcome to the department of bioscience bioengineering the department of bioscience bioengineering at iit jodhpur established in offers tech
program in bioengineering an tech
and tech ph dual degree programs in bioscience bioengineering
our programs are uniquely designed to inspire innovations in biotechnology engineering and medicine from an understanding of biological systems
this capacity building is assuming importance in the present times for providing solutions to societal problems in trans disciplinary frameworks
major focus is on neurosciences and neurodegenerative disorders mechanistic biology environmental and medical microbiology computational biology precision health and bioprospecting for novel molecules and bio actives from natural resources of the thar ecoregion
we also offer competitive ph program to inculcate scientific temper independent thinking and skillsets for scientific entrepreneurship
we encourage and provide avenues for the participation of bioengineering graduates with diverse engineering disciplines to develop technologies for affordable and smart healthcare clean environment precision medicine and health sustainable and affordable energy faculties at the department with wide ranging expertise conduct fundamental and translational research in focus areas that are contemporary and aligned to major sustainable development goals
we are passionate about research and actively communicate our science through regular outreach programs

prof neha jain acting head bsbe email phone bioscience and bioengineering are pivotal to india growth
driving innovation and advancing technology in healthcare
agriculture and environmental sustainability
the department of bioscience bioengineering at iit jodhpur plays key role in implementing diverse advanced biological research areas within these fields
with strong focus on fostering research and development and entrepreneurship
by offering multidisciplinary platform that integrates cutting edge research in drug discovery genomics and affordable and integrative precision healthcare solutions
big data biology and bioinformatics
the department actively promotes entrepreneurship by providing incubation support facilitating industry collaborations and encouraging the growth of startup ventures in biosciences
with access to advanced research facilities and guidance from expert faculty members students can transform their ideas into practical marketable solutions benefiting industries such as healthcare agriculture and biomanufacturing
bsbe department iit jodhpur emphasizes interdisciplinary research in the near future which may also align with national initiatives like the bioe3 policy to promote commercialization and the establishment of bioeconomy hubs
the curriculum is thoughtfully designed to meet the diverse aspirations of students entering the fields of biosciences and bioengineering
this approach not only strengthens the academic peers ecosystem but also creates employment opportunities and enhances societal well being

civil and infrastructure engineering iit jodhpur
welcome to the civil and infrastructure engineering the rapidly urbanizing society and increasing quality of life demand reliable and intelligent infrastructure systems which are capable of catering to the societal needs at different scales from an individual to the community level
consequently the civil and infrastructure industry has undergone profound changes and is constantly evolving
however the new age designs and innovations in the civil and infrastructure industry can only be driven by group of engineering graduates having multidisciplinary training and sound understanding of emerging technologies
the department of civil and infrastructure engineering iit jodhpur esatablished in january
we started academics programs in the academic year
the department offers unique programs that incorporate and integrate the elements of conventional civil engineering with advanced transformative technologies such as artificial intelligence ai cyber physical systems cps digital twins dt and automated management and information systems
additionally major thrust is also planned on the design implementation and maintenance of large scale integrated infrastructure systems across different domains
the focus areas of the department are energy and environment building sciences safety and services applications of ai iot and cps in civil and infrastructure engineering smart and integrated infrastructure urban architecture and planning construction technology and management transportation engineering structural engineering geotechnical engineering water resources engineering engineering geology vision the department strives to be an internationally renowned centre of excellence in education research and innovation with major focus on sustainability and digitalization of infrastructure
missions develop unique courses in the field of civil and infrastructure engineering through advanced ug and pg programs with multidisciplinary perspective
establish high quality teaching and research facilities in the areas of smart resilient and sustainable infrastructure engineering to foster the needs of society
promote consilient research through application of ai ml cyber physical systems digital twin and advanced visualization in the areas of energy environment transportation and construction
promote innovative and entrepreneurial activities through intellectual property generation and transfer
nurture the potential of students to become future leaders in the civil engineering industry for leadership achievements scientific breakthroughs and social welfare

postgraduate programs computer science engineering iit jodhpur
postgraduate programs tech
computer science and engineering cse the tech program in computer science and engineering cse at iit jodhpur establishes balance between traditional and emerging areas of the discipline by offering students combined knowledge of theory and practice
the program is designed to extend the undergraduate computing skills with up to date and in depth expertise in specialized areas of the computer science discipline
the curriculum is developed in consultation with experts from academia and industry to address the needs of both domains and open wide range of possibilities for students for their future endeavors
students are equipped with relevant concepts algorithms techniques and underpinning principles while developing the ability to solve real world problems in number of application areas such as computer systems and security distributed computing internet of things artificial intelligence and big data

artificial intelligence ai the tech
in artificial intelligence ai aims to fill the critical needs of the industry and to fill the gap in the availability of highly skilled ai scientists and engineers
the major goal of the two year tech
ai the program is to impart rigorous training in strong foundations of artificial intelligence as well as cutting edge ai tools and techniques and produce graduates who can reinforce the country efforts towards developing ai technologies

cse download file kb regulations for postgraduate programs download file kb dual master with university of albany suny ua the dual mater program in cse and cybersecurity in collaboration of university at albany suny ualbany
this is unique program that leverages the strengths of the two institutions iit jodhpur in cse and ualbany in cybersecurity
the field of cybersecurity is becoming more critical as we become more technology dependent and there is short supply of cybersecurity professionals in the united states of the order of quarter million jobs
also the field is growing rapidly
students will get an mtech in cse and an ms in cyber security
ualbany is providing dollar scholarship for our students since we are partner institution
additionally this program is stem certified in the united states which means that students will automatically get year work visa at the end of their one year at ualbany
also currently there are over quarter million open jobs in the cybersecurity
the previous set of students that have gone from iit jodhpur sme we have had placement in the industry or at education institutions for doctoral programs with full assistantships
almost all students have also been able to take assistantships at ualbany making their education even more affordable
tech ph dual degree cse tech ph dual degree ai iit jodhpur has been offering mtech phd dual degree programs since july
these programs have been designed particularly for students with an inclination for research
students who have completed their tech
are eligible to join this program
students complete their tech
course work and then start their research and earn both tech
and ph degrees at the end of the program
there are two distinct aspects of these programs first it provides an avenue for students to earn both degrees in far lesser duration compared to if these were done individually and second students are awarded fellowship equivalent to that of jrf from the date of their joining
academically these programs facilitate students to decide upon their area of research by the end of the first semester
after phase of rigorous coursework in the first two years they start working towards the academic requirements of phd that includes comprehensive exam state of the art seminar publications in top conferences and journals and thesis
the students can also work on industry projects and collaborate with faculty from other academic research bodies
this program offers the benefit of phd level scholarship from the first year itself and the integrated courses and research helps in reducing the overall time of completing mtech and phd
the department of cse is currently offering two such programs mtech phd dual degree in cse and mtech phd dual degree in ai and is continuously looking for motivated and capable students to conduct advanced research in all areas of computer science and ai please visit the research page for more details
the coursework requirements of these programs are similar to the corresponding mtech programs and other support travel support institute mobility grant fellowship schemes in addition to that from the ministry of education etc
and research related academic requirements are similar to the phd program

seminars and meetings physics iit jodhpur
seminars and meetings diving deep into physics colloquium series from empirical to machine learning potentials modeling liquid ethylene glycol by dr anjali gaur jncasr bengaluru india on jan at pm ist title of the talk from empirical to machine learning potentials modeling liquid ethylene glycol speaker dr anjali gaur jncasr bengaluru when
jan from ist where
kothari seminar hall department of physics
abstract refining empirical force field ff parameters for accurate structural and dynamic properties is challenging especially for complex systems like ethylene glycol eg
even for very simple system like water there are over ffs available and each has its benefits and drawbacks
refined ff enables studying chemical systems over nanosecond to microsecond timescales unlike ab initio molecular dynamics md which is limited to few 10s of picoseconds
combining gas phase dft calculations ab initio md and classical md simulations we developed an empirical ff that accurately matches the liquid structure and dynamics of eg with experimental data
additionally interesting conformational changes of eg molecules in its aqueous solutions and at liquid vapor interfaces were observed
however traditional ffs fail to capture electronic polarization limiting their ability to reproduce properties like dipole moment and dielectric constant
to address this machine learned potentials trained on bulk quantum dft data offer more accurate alternative without the need for manual parameterization
given the simplicity and efficacy of the potential models presented here they are ideal for investigating mixtures of eg with other fluids including deep eutectic solvents and anti freezing solutions
pathirannahalage et al chem
visit link gaur and balasubramanian phys
visit link gaur and balasubramanian chemistryopen e202200132
visit link gaur and balasubramanian langmuir
visit link about the speaker dr anjali gaur completed her ph under the guidance of prof balasubramanian sundaram at jncasr bengaluru
her doctoral research focused on force field development for liquids and investigating their physical properties using ab initio and classical molecular dynamics simulations
currently she is working on the development of machine learning potentials for liquid ethylene glycol
as postdoctoral researcher she will join the phenix cnrs lab to work with prof mathieu salanne on the development of machine learning based classical force fields for battery electrolytes
all are welcome

artificial intelligence of things aiot fab facility iit jodhpur
welcome to the artificial intelligence of things aiot fab facility artificial intelligence of things aiot is new technology vertical that has leveraged the convergence of artificial intelligence ai the internet of things iot along with mobile communication technologies and has led to massive growth in the intelligent devices that are connected together to solve problems collaboratively
artificial intelligence of things aiot initiative at iit jodhpur aims to create facilities for end to end design development prototyping and delivery of aiot systems for start ups msmes and end user industries catering to applications of industry agriculture healthcare infrastructure energy environment and water management
two important facilities are created for the development of aiot technologies by faculty and students of iit jodhpur with participation of industry and other academic partners aiot fab facility the fab is equipped with set of instruments for the development characterization of sensors of various kinds that are the foundation of an iot ecosystem providing devices with the ability to collect data
the fab facility is undergoing further updates with the addition of new instruments
the aiot fab also focuses on the development of new sensing materials and targets applications based on photonics biotechnology and solar cells
it also envisions developing flexible sensors that can be used for making wearable devices along with other inertial sensors utilising mems technology
aiot electronic manufacturing unit emu the aiot emu facility is to be equipped with eda cad software for designing targeted sensors signal conditioning interface circuit fabless vlsi fpga design pcb design
the emu facility will also house chip assembly packaging electronic component assembly along with hardware and software platforms that are required for developing iot enabled ai applications
the emu will also facilitate the commercialization of products with facilities of prototyping limited manufacturing and pilot production in line with stakeholders strategy direction
the aiot initiative at iit jodhpur is also supported by the aiot innovation hub foundation ihf which is working as joint venture section company between risl rajcomp info services ltd jckif jodhpur city knowledge and innovation foundation and iit jodhpur
vision to be nationally leading hub for innovation research and education in artificial intelligence of things aiot by integrating advanced semiconductor fabrication smart sensor systems and intelligent computing
the aiot fab facility envisions enabling the next generation of intelligent connected technologies empowering academia startups and industry with state of the art infrastructure for design prototyping and testing
we aspire to drive transformative impact through development of indigenous smart sensor technologies including gas sensors biosensors and neuromorphic devices
scalable prototyping of mems nems electronics
cross disciplinary collaboration bridging materials science electronics ai and iot
serving as training and incubation ground for future technologists and researchers in india aiot ecosystem
our long term goal is to catalyze self reliant semiconductor and sensor ecosystem in india aligned with national missions such as digital india make in india and semiconductor mission
the aiot fab artificial intelligence of things fabrication facility is part of the aiot innovation hub at iit jodhpur
it is state of the art facility for developing fabricating and deploying advanced aiot systems
equipped with tools for creating mems sensors associated electronics and other intelligent systems
provides an ecosystem for faculty students and industry partners to co create aiot technologies and products

research highlights mathematics iit jodhpur
research highlights general casorati inequalities and implications for riemannian maps and riemannian submersions title general casorati inequalities and implications for riemannian maps and riemannian submersions author name ravindra singh kiran meena kapish chand meena journal name journal of mathematical analysis and applications publisher elsevier link doi year abstract this paper presents general forms of casorati inequalities for riemannian maps and riemannian submersions between riemannian manifolds
using these general forms we obtain casorati inequalities for riemannian maps resp
submersions whose target resp
source spaces are generalized complex and generalized sasakian space forms
as consequence we give casorati inequalities for riemannian maps resp
submersions when the target resp
source spaces are real complex real hler sasakian kenmotsu cosymplectic and almost space forms
to support these general forms in the particular cases when the target or source spaces are real complex sasakian and kenmotsu space forms we verify known casorati inequalities for riemannian maps and riemannian submersions
further we give casorati inequalities for invariant and anti invariant riemannian maps resp
submersions whose target resp
source spaces are generalized complex and generalized sasakian space forms
toward information on geometric characteristics we discuss the equality cases
we also exemplify the general forms

collaborations chemistry iit jodhpur
collaborations faculty members of the department of chemistry are keen to collaborate with individuals from academia laboratories and industry in india and abroad in areas of mutual interest
these collaborations include teaching activities authoring books and review articles organising national and international visits for undertaking short courses and research work joint mous for degree programs research facilities etc
undertaking research projects and other research activities developing technologies through consultancy projects with industries the department of chemistry iit jodhpur is in collaboration with industrial partners as well as top universities in india and internationally
some activities at glance department of chemistry iit jodhpur organized gian programme with prof mukundan thelakkat univeristy of bayreuth germany department of chemistry and chemical engineering iit jodhpur signed loi for joint research and phd degree with kaohsiung medical university taiwan
besides the academic program and research work we also carry out instrumental measurements chemical analysis research training and consultancy work for industrial academic other research organizations as well as with individuals expressing such an interest
our technical staff team is well trained experienced skilled and capable of dealing with diverse analytical and chemical characterization tools
we therefore invite all who are interested in our work and potential cooperation to contact us for further information and details

news and newsletter chemistry iit jodhpur
news and newsletter congratulations to dr sandip murarka for receiving the crsi bronze medal dr sandip murarka has been conferred the prestigious crsi bronze medal by the chemical research society of india crsi at the recently concluded crsi national symposium in chemistry held at dibrugarh university
this distinguished recognition acknowledges his significant contributions to the fields of organic synthesis and catalysis
iit jodhpur congratulates dr murarka on this well deserved honor and wishes him continued success in his future endeavors

latest events mathematics iit jodhpur
latest events invited talk by prof basant lal sharma on january at 12pm speaker prof basant lal sharma from the dept
of mechanical engineering iit kanpur title of the talk on analysis of some direct and inverse problems in scattering involving lattices
date time venue jan at pm on seminar room dept
of mathematics abstract in this talk will give brief overview of physical problems involving crystal lattices that lead to certain idealized cases which permit an interesting application of mathematics
will mainly focus on those applications where fourier analysis wiener hopf factorization theory of inverse problems and stochastic calculus play dominant role
in such an overview of several works available in public domain will touch upon topic just enough to highlight connection with mathematics and also point out some open problems
however may not be able to go into details but discussions after seminar can be used to get into details
will start with the problem of scattering of waves by sharp slits in crystal lattices which can be understood in the paradigm of mechanical waves as well as tight binding approximations in quantum mechanics
some of these problems involve issues related to matrix wiener hopf factorization and are currently open
will also talk about some recent works dealing with the inverse problems for such discrete models based on the observations of scattering data
will discuss briefly some ways to capture effects of stochasticity in lattice model specifications and obtain analogues of certain rigorous results available in the continuous framework
useful refs see complete list here sharma bl diffraction of waves on square lattice by semi infinite crack siam journal on applied mathematics
sharma bl near tip field for diffraction on square lattice by crack siam journal on applied mathematics
sharma bl wave propagation in bifurcated waveguides of square lattice strips siam journal on applied mathematics
sharma bl electronic transport across junction between armchair graphene nanotube and zigzag nanoribbon the european physical journal
novikov rg sharma bl inverse source problem for discrete helmholtz equation inverse problems
garnier sharma bl surface waves in randomly perturbed discrete models siam multiscale modeling simulation

message from the head of the department assoc
prof neha jain acting head bsbe email phone bioscience and bioengineering are pivotal to india growth
driving innovation and advancing technology in healthcare
agriculture and environmental sustainability
the department of bioscience bioengineering at iit jodhpur plays key role in implementing diverse advanced biological research areas within these fields
with strong focus on fostering research and development and entrepreneurship
by offering multidisciplinary platform that integrates cutting edge research in drug discovery genomics and affordable and integrative precision healthcare solutions
big data biology and bioinformatics
the department actively promotes entrepreneurship by providing incubation support facilitating industry collaborations and encouraging the growth of startup ventures in biosciences
with access to advanced research facilities and guidance from expert faculty members students can transform their ideas into practical marketable solutions benefiting industries such as healthcare agriculture and biomanufacturing
bsbe department iit jodhpur emphasizes interdisciplinary research in the near future which may also align with national initiatives like the bioe3 policy to promote commercialization and the establishment of bioeconomy hubs
the curriculum is thoughtfully designed to meet the diverse aspirations of students entering the fields of biosciences and bioengineering
this approach not only strengthens the academic peers ecosystem but also creates employment opportunities and enhances societal well being
jul feb feb

completed projects chemistry iit jodhpur
completed projects faculty members of the department have completed the following sponsored research projects towards the development of low cost water quality sensors phase ii panasonic rakesh kumar sharma pi slow relaxations of chemically confined water near membranes ananya debnath pi the slow relaxations are found to be universal irrespective of the nature of chemical confinement as the underlying source is dynamical heterogeneities at physiological temperature
publications srivastava debnath
hydration dynamics of lipid membrane hydrogen bond networks and lipid lipid associations
the journal of chemical physics
issn srivastava malik debnath
heterogeneity in structure and dynamics of water near bilayers using tip3p and tip4p water models
issn srivastava karmakar debnath
quantification of spatio temporal scales of dynamical heterogeneity of water near lipid membranes above supercooling
phase transition of surfactants science and engineering research board dst government of india ananya debnath pi surfactants are amphilic molecules which can self assemble into different macro aggregates with huge applications in cosmetic industry oil recovery process etc
designing phase for the self assembled surfactants is important to target functionality relevant for industry
we find that water concentration can be controlling parameter for micelle to lamellar phase transition using all atom molecular dynamics aa md simulations
using multi scale modeling we find that trans bilayer compositional asymmetry can induce interdigitated gel to ripple phase transition at the same temperature and surfactant to cosurfactant ratio
these phases are particularly important for formulating hair conditioners hair gels etc
project image publications lunkad srivastava debnath
influence of water concentrations on the phase transformation of model surfactant co surfactant water system
issn srivastava debnath
cylindrical to spherical shape transformations of micelles using all atom and coarse grained molecular dynamics simulations aip conference proceedings
role of non covalent forces in peptide based soft materials ananya debnath pi non covalent forces are responsible for the association of peptidebased hydrogels which are biologically inspired and have applications in material sciences technology
we focus on understanding the driving forces behind the self assembled super structures
integrated information from lectronic structure calculations and md simulations provide insights on the preferential building blocks of self assembled structures and the underlying physical phenomena driving water confinement within hydrogels with bio medical applications sci rep chem sci bul
project image publications ahmed pramanik sankar srivastava singha dowari srivastava mohanta debnath
solvent assisted tuning of morphology of peptide perylenediimide conjugate helical fibers to nano rings and their differential semiconductivity
issn singha srivastava pramanik ahmed dowari chowdhuri das debnath
unusual confinement properties of water insoluble small peptide hydrogel
issn srivastava garg das debnath
molecular dynamics simulations of stacked conjugated soft material binding energy and preferential geometry for self assembly invited article on bulletin of materials science for the thematic issue on soft materials in press
chemical dynamics simulations of complex organic reactions mechanistic insights and microsolvation effects science and engineering research board dst government of india manikandan paranjothy pi project image publications krishnan sharma lourderaj and paranjothy classical dynamics simulations of dissociation of protonated tryptophan in the gas phase phys
krishnan vincent and paranjothy classical dynamics simulations of interstellar glycine formation via ch2 nh co h2o reaction chem
godara verma and paranjothy dissociation chemistry of oxetanone in the gas phase phys
solid state high energy density lithium ion rechargeable batteries indo portuguese research cooperation in science technology rakesh kumar sharma pi the project is towards the development of fast charging and high energy density li ion rechargeable batteries where iit jodhpur and the university of minho portugal together developed efficient cathode materials polymer separators stable at higher voltages volts
the cathode materials are prepared via surface modification and doping lanthanides in lmo and the separator is produced as porous membranes based on polar polymers
based on performance coil cells are fabricated and characterization for various field applications
project image publications ram patel singhal choudhary
and sharma on the study of mixing and drying on electrochemical performance of spinel limn2o4 cathodes journal of renewable and sustainable energy the american institute of physics vol
pp ram singhal and sharma
preliminary study of dysprosium doped limn2o4 spinel cathode materials materials today proceedings elsevier vol
pp ram singhal choudhary and sharma on the key role of dy3 in spinel limn2o4 cathodes for li ion rechargeable batteries journal of electroanalytical chemistry elsevier vol
pp ram goren ferdov silva singhal costa sharma
and lanceros mendez synthesis and improved electrochemical performance of limn2xgdxo4 based cathodes solid state ionics elsevier vol
pp pura ferdov silva singhal costa sharma
and lanceros mendez improved performance of rare earth doped limn2o4 cathodes for lithium ion battery applications new journal of chemistry royal society of chemistry pp ram sharma and singhal synthesis physical and impedance spectroscopy of tb iii doped limn2o4 cathode material for lithium ion rechargeable batteries journal of advanced electrochemistry american scientific publishers vol pp
towards the development of low cost water quality sensors phase panasonic rakesh kumar sharma pi
generation storage and distribution of solar hydrogen department of science technology dst government of india rakesh kumar sharma pi in this project we have developed surfactant free facile synthesis method for titania tio2 nanostructures by which morphology and phase can be tuned through temperature variation
the formation of oval shaped nanocrystals and nanorods at different temperatures are attributed to the phase transformation between anatase and rutile by one step chemical reaction process involving titanium tetraisopropoxide and aqueous ethyl alcohol
categorically two different morphologies of tio2 were observed with respect to variation in temperature with nanorods formation at and and oval shape formation at oc oc and oc
crystalline nature and size of tio2 nanoparticles were determined by transmission electron microscopy tem and ray diffraction xrd
the energy gap of tio2 nanoparticles was determined by optical absorption measurement and found to be in the range of to ev with an indirect band nature
additionally we have developed highly efficient visible light mo abx3 cds nano composites for photocatalytic hydrogen production via water splitting and block elements examples under sunlight
project image publications kiran shejale devika laishram rakesh sharma and arun kumar photocatalytic nanocomposite and applications thereof application date march shejale laishram and sharma engineered zno tio2 nanospheres for high performing membrane assimilated photocatalytic water remediation and energy harvesting chemistryselect wiley pp devika shejale gupta and sharma heterostructured hfo2 tio2 nano spheroids for visible photocatalytic water remediation material letters elsevier pp shejale laishram gupta and sharma zinc oxide titania heterojunction based solid nanospheres as photoanodes for electron trapping in dye sensitized solar cells energy technology wiley vol
pp laishram shejale sharma and gupta hfo2 nanodots incorporated in tio2 and its hydrogenation for high performance dye sensitized solar cells rsc advances royal society of chemistry pp shejale laishram and sharma high performance dye sensitized solar cell using dimensionally controlled titania synthesized at sub zero temperatures rsc advances royal society of chemistry pp shejale laishram roy kumar and sharma on the study of phase and dimensionally controlled titania nanostructures synthesis at sub zero temperatures materials design elsevier vol
asymmetric hydrogenation on carbon nanotube surface dst rfbr indo russian bilateral project rakesh kumar sharma pi in this project we have developed highly selective novel heterogeneous nanocatalytic systems based on carbon materials and polymers
out of various carbon supported metals the cinchona pt mwcnts is found to be most efficient system and provides high enantioselectivity ee due to the high absorption of cinchona and atomically dispersed pt nanoparticles that maximize metal utilization
substrate modifier interaction is examined by time dependent nmr studies
during this study the excellent activity of pt plane is observed due to higher packing density stability and cohesive energy than other crystal planes leading to significant chemisorption of variety of chemical entities
for that strategy is developed that involves the preparation of pt from phase transfer reagent tw20 followed by mixing of functionalized carbon materials to obtain heterogeneous pt hexagonal nanocrystal loaded carbon catalyst by simple thermolytic reduction in air which are tested for asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoester at ambient reaction conditions using cinchonine as modifier
chiral polymers are prepared and used as tunable supports it is found that the chirality could be transferred from the support to substrate with ease in case of hydrogenation of carbonyl groups
project image publications sharma and sharma electrophilic catalysed asymmetric allylation of diphenylmethanimine chirality wiley vol
pp nunes pereiraa sharma fernandes oliveira moreira sharma and lanceros mendez poly vinylidene fluoride composites with carbon nanotubes decorated with metal nanoparticles composites part engineering elsevier pp
sharma and sharma platinum graphene as recyclable catalyst for asymmetric hydrogenation of alpha ketoesters catalysis in green chemistry and engineering begell house vol
pp doi sharma and sharma platinum functionalized chiral polyamides efficient heterogeneous catalyst for solvent free asymmetric hydrogenation of ethyl oxo phenylbutanoate chemistryselect wiley vol
pp sharma and sharma asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoesters on pt surface new journal of chemistry royal society of chemistry pp sharma and sharma platinum functionalized multiwall carbon nanotube composites as recyclable catalyst for highly efficient asymmetric hydrogenation of methyl pyruvate rsc advances royal society of chemistry pp sharma and sharma comparative study of pt loaded carbon support catalysis in hydrogenation reaction chemical engineering and chemical processes global science and technology forum vol pp
molecular sensors synthesis and anion recognition studies science and engineering research board dst government of india rakesh kumar sharma pi in this project new calix and cucurbit based supramolecular colourimetric sensors for selective recognition of toxic aquatic anions
approximately odd sensors were developed and they are selective towards anions including inorganic organic drugs and vitamins
the arsenic fluoride and ascorbate are few that give sharp colour changes
project image publications nunes pereiraa lima choudhary sharma ferdov botelho sharma and lanceros mendez highly efficient removal of fluoride from aqueous media through polymer composite membranes separation and purification technology elsevier vol
soni pandey choudhary plappally and sharma dipicrylhydrazine versatile visual anions sensor journal of environmental chemical engineering elsevier vol
pp pandey soni choudhary sharma and sharma understanding behavior of vitamin guest binding with the cucurbit uril host supramolecular chemistry taylor and francis vol
pp sharma choudhary satankar plappally sharma development of low cost polycalixarene clay based water guards iww ministry of water resources goi vol pp choudhary sharma and plappally local material composite sintered systems for fluoride removal desalination and water treatment taylor and francis vol pp important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur

weekly hours of live online training and hours of self guided learning programming foundations databases with mysql maths refrehser cloud foundations gen ai agentic ai and prompt engineering iit jodhpur ers1ons campus immersion will be an essential element of the program experience during which learners are required to attend campus activities and sessions at the iit jodhpur campus
there will be one immersion per academic year and will be conducted for to days
attending one immersion is mandatory for students
learners will get live classroom experience with the opportunity to meet and learn in person from the es teemed faculty at iit jodhpur and engage in peer learning or presentations and project work
the immersion timetable will be set by iit jodhpur and will be communicated to students in advance post onset of the pro gram
the policies and requirements regarding campus immersion including the number of immersions student must attend are subject to review and final approval by iit jodhpur
while it is currently planned that students must attend at least one immersion program per academic year these policies may be revised based on re source availability at iit jodhpur
any changes will be communicated according ly
the institution reserves the right to modify update or amend the immersion attendance requirements based on academic administrative or regulatory con siderations

message from the director our present age requires creative solutions to pressing problems of energy environment sustainable development and global well being
this means thinking out of the box and moving away from traditional model of education which rewards rote learning and kills the innate creativity of students
therefore our vision is to equip learners in the pgd tech in artificial intelligence course with the necessary modern skills and competencies to participate in rapidly transforming global knowledge ecosystem that is being shaped drastically by technologies such as artificial intelligence
prof avinash kumar agarwal is currently the director at iit jodhpur
prior to joining iit jodhpur he served as an sbi endowed chair professor and bose national fellow at the department of mechanical engineering iit kanpur
his research interests include ic engines combustion alternative fuels optical diagnostics particulate and emission control etc
prof agarwal has published over peer reviewed international journal and conference papers edited books book chapters attracting more than scopus and more than google scholar citations
he is the associate principal editor of prestigious fuel journal
for his outstanding contributions prof agarwal has been conferred upon several prominent recognitions and awards such as bose national fellowship by serb the clarivate analytics india citation award in engineering and technology and prestigious shanti swarup bhatnagar prize in engineering sciences
he is an elected fellow of the society of automotive engineers international usa american society of mechanical engineers indian national academy of engineering royal society of chemistry national academy of science allahabad american association for advancement in science usa combustion institute usa and the world academy of sciences
are you ready to take these opportunities to accelerate your career
the demand for skilled professio nals in this space is skyrocketing
the global al market is now projected to reach staggering us trillion by representing an immense and sustained period of growth
india demand for al professionals is projected to hit million skilled individuals by highlighting the coun try position as global al talent hub
the indian al market is expected to witness explosive and sustained growth with some reports forecasting market size of billion by and broader digital economy contribution of up to billion by
boston consulting group nasscom
the demand for skilled al professionals has not just skyrocketed it has become one of the defining forces of the modern job market
with the rapid integration of al and generative al across every sector from finance and healthcare to manufac turing and marketing the need for experts who can design develop and deploy intelligent systems is at an all time high
this program is designed to equip you with the advanced skills required to lead this transformation
more reasons to be part of al revolution
1_ _ii fast growing field one of the most fascinating and quickly growing topics in modern times is artificial intelligence
it is at the forefront of technological development and wide range of industries including manufacturing transportation healthcare and entertainment can use it
there are many job prospects as result of the ongoing increase in demand for al professionals
high demand and competitive salaries there is lack of qualified al practitioners as result of the demand for al professionals surpassing the talent pool
for individuals who choose to enter the area the lack of al competence translates into competitive pay and alluring compensation packages
al experts frequently have above average pay and fantastic opportunities for professional advancement
global significance and effect artificial intelligence is global phenomenon and its developments and uses are being investigated globally
working on foreign projects and collaborating with individuals from other nations and cultures can be two of the many chances that career in artificial intelligence can provide
continuous career growth al is still in its early stages and the potential for career growth and professional development is immense
as you gain experience and expertise in al you can progress to more specialized roles lead teams engage in research or even contribute to shaping the future of al through academia or industry leadership positions
about the pg diploma and tech in al this tech in al at iit jodhpur will offer students with deep knowledge of both fundamen tal al technologies as well as application oriented al
student completing this program will be capable to undertake careers in industry as well as academia
he she will have the option to explore variety of domains including manufacturing fintech healthcare agriculture food processing education retail customer engagement human and robot interaction intelligent automation smart city aid for differently abled accessibility technology

jal application focused learning from top industry leaders

experimental and lmmersive learning the program integrates industry use cases mini projects assignments and capstone projects
this hands on approach ensures that students are well prepared to apply their knowledge in real world scenarios

admission to tech program delivered in executive mod is based on the discretion of the program director

note the electives list above is indicative
iit jodhpur is an institution of eminence in areas of emerging technologies and launches new courses dynamically every trimester
therefore the compulsory electives are updated on regular basis and will be made available as per discretion of the faculty and program directors campuslmm experience being an iitian with campus immersions campus immersion will be an essential element of the program experience during which learners are required to attend campus activities and sessions at the iit jodhpur campus
there will be one immersion per academic year and will be conducted for to days
attending one immersion is mandatory for students
learners will get live classroom experience with the opportunity to meet and learn in person from the es teemed faculty at iit jodhpur and engage in peer learning or presentati ons and project work
the immersion timetable will be set by iit jodhpur and will be communicated to students in advance post onset of the pro gram
the policies and requirements regarding campus immersion including the number of immersions student must attend are subject to review and final approval by iit jodhpur
while it is currently planned that students must attend at least one immersion program per academic year these policies may be revised based on re source availability at iit jodhpur
any changes will be communicated accordingly
the institution reserves the right to modify update or amend the immersion attendance requirements based on academic administrative or regulatory considerations
eb duration months hours programme commencement january tentative programme fee course fee including application processing fees the application processing fee is included in and adjusted with the course fees which must be paid at the time of completing the application
if the application is not accepted by iit jodhpur or futurense this amount will be fully refunded
however if the student withdraws or drops out for any reason after the application is accepted this amount is strictly non refundable
administrativ fee this includes lodging meals excluded and is separate from course fees
no refunds post the last date of registration
fees are subject to change based on the institute current policy

structural engineering civil and infrastructure engineering iit jodhpur
structural engineering department of civil and infrastructural engineering iit jodhpur plans to establish state of the art laboratory in the structural engineering domain
further it also plans to establish various theme based laboratories such as structural health monitoring laboratory structural modelling and simulation laboratory risk reliability and stochastic modelling laboratory wind energy and engineering laboratory steel and composite structures laboratory and earthquake engineering laboratory
it involves studying ambient vibrations conducting experimental studies on models prototypes of structural elements and assemblies under various static and dynamic loading conditions and providing technical services for testing and researching the structural behavior and properties of materials
these laboratories envision to boost the research and teaching ecosystem in the structure engineering and interdisciplinary domains
at present the structural engineer laboratory caters experiments related to non destructive testing such as rebound hammer ultrasonic pulse velocity rebar locator half cell potentiometer maxwell reciprocal theorem apparatus two and three hinged arch apparatus suspension bridge apparatus pin jointed truss apparatus elastic properties of deflected beam apparatus behaviour of columns and struts apparatus elastically coupled beam apparatus and unsymmetrical bending apparatus
at present the laboratory is equipped with two servo controlled hydraulic testing machines kn universal testing machine utm and kn compression testing machine ctm
additionally the laboratory has an channel universal amplifier hbk make kn flexure testing machine ftm and various other equipment suitable for undergraduate level laboratory work
the laboratory also has multiple software platforms for numerical modelling and simulation such as abaqus staad pro and sap
500kn compression testing machine kn fatigue testing machine universal channel data acquisition system daq thyristor based furnace 500kn compression testing machine the kn high force fatigue testing machines are servo hydraulic systems equipped with large slot bed and high stiffness four column load frame specifically designed for high force testing of materials and components
these machines are widely used for both static and fatigue testing in applications such as tension compression fracture fatigue crack propagation high cycle fatigue hcf elastomer property evaluation and flexural strength analysis
common test specimens include metals wire ropes rebar rails concrete and aerospace components
operating in stroke control mode the system maintains the load within user defined limits during specimen loading
key features include mm 75mm actuator stroke with mm resolution capability for cyclic operation customizable hydraulic and dynamic performance to suit specific applications and an adjustable upper crosshead with hydraulic lifts and locks for quick and easy daylight adjustment
kn fatigue testing machine the kn series servo hydraulic test systems are designed to meet nearly all requirements for testing the strength durability and toughness of metals composites and plastics
these systems are supported by wide range of transducers application software and accessories to accommodate standard testing procedures
key features include double acting servo hydraulic actuator with force capacities up to kn kip high stiffness precision aligned self reacting twin column load frame with the actuator positioned in the lower base or top crosshead and usable stroke length of mm 75mm
the system is rated for cyclic operations and includes mechanical hydraulic and pneumatic anti vibration pads for enhanced stability
users can tailor the hydraulic configuration and dynamic performance to specific application needs
the adjustable upper crosshead is equipped with hydraulic lifts and locks for easy daylighting
the system is compatible with broad selection of grips fixtures chambers video extensometers protective shields and other accessories
universal channel data acquisition system daq the hbm quantumx mx840b is an advanced channel universal data logger that allows the connection of sensors and transducers based on different technologies across all its channels
with the support of hbm innovative plug measure technology the module automatically recognizes connected transducers streamlining setup and ensuring accurate measurements
each of the eight channels can accommodate wide range of sensor types including strain gauges in half or full bridge configurations dc or cf with khz voltage inputs mv and current fed piezos iepe icp piezoresistive full bridges thermocouples types and resistance thermometers pt100 pt1000 resistance up to ohm potentiometric transducers inductive half or full bridge sensors including lvdts and current inputs ma
additionally up to four channels support rotary encoders counters frequency rpm measurements and the ssi protocol
the module features high resolution bit analog to digital converter and offers sample rate of up to ks per channel complemented by active low pass filters
renowned for its precision the mx840b achieves an accuracy class of up to making it ideal for high performance measurement applications
thyristor based furnace the horizontal muffle furnace is high temperature thermal processing unit available in the lab designed for material heating sintering and thermal treatment applications
it features robust construction with hinged door for safe and easy access and is mounted on sturdy mild steel stand with the control panel positioned below for user convenience
the furnace offers compact working chamber of mm mm mm and can operate at temperatures up to
it uses durable apm grade kanthal coils as heating elements and type thermocouple to ensure precise temperature monitoring with accuracy
programmable pid controller program steps enables accurate temperature control while solid state thyristor regulates power
the chamber is well insulated with combination of high temperature bricks and zirconia blended alumina ceramic fiber blankets enhancing thermal efficiency and safety
the system operates on phase power supply and consumes between to kw
additional features include analog voltage and current meters high quality switch gears and safety mechanisms like slow blow fuse and user settable over temperature protection

engineering science office of academics affairs iit jodhpur
engineering science no iitj oa dated july tech
in engineering science tech
in engineering science message from chairperson es undergratuate commitee institute background and vision program background engineering science options student details domain options for tech
es cs chem cse ee ai ee mech electrical civil engineering ai material science ai bio ai curriculum first and second semester third and fourth semester engineering science undergraduate committee student details tech
in engineering science back to index message from chairperson es undergratuate commitee back to index engineering applications generally require multidisciplinary knowledge spanning across various disciplines of engineering and sciences
knowledge in humanities along with engineering and or sciences are also finding important applications
in engineering sciences es is unique bachelors degree program of iit jodhpur which aim to impart core knowledge in two targeted focus areas in science and engineering to meet demands of emerging industrial and academic requirements
any student admitted to iit jodhpur can opt for tech
in engineering science post 1st year
students will be required to complete core courses of es in the first two years of study whereas they can outline their own curriculum in the guidance of faculty advisor in the form of electives for the remaining two years from courses across disciplines of engineering science and humanities
in line with regular tech
programs students can also use credits out of stipulated electives and additional credits to earn minor specialization as an add on to the tech
the flexibility of curriculum selection as per students capabilities and interest with faculty guidance aims to impart sufficient knowledge to the students to be future ready professionals
professor ajay agarwal chairperson institute background and vision back to index innovations scientific discoveries and new technologies are the essential foundations of the indian institute of technology jodhpur
the institute focuses on building solid and in depth knowledge in the engineering domains and strongly encourages interdisciplinary learning
the courses offered are defined such that it enhances the imagination and creativity of the students to identify and solve new problems in society
our endeavour is to build an atmosphere for uninhibited ideas the institute offers tech programs computer science engineering electrical engineering engineering sciences artificial intelligence and data science mechanical engineering bioengineering materials engineering chemical engineering and civil and infrastructure engineering
the institute also empowers students by allowing them to avail specializations minors and elective courses
program background back to index engineering science is unique branch of engineering designed to cater to the upcoming demand of the industry and research training the best of students to have strong foundation in every field along with specialization in their topic of interest
our institute is one of the primary institutes offering an engineering science program
this programme is four year course
the first two years are devoted to topics which we believe all engineering undergraduates should study
in the third and fourth years the students have the opportunity to study any two complementary disciplines by drafting their own course structure
this fosters multi disciplinary thinking and integrated application of engineering scientific and mathematical principles in young students
the students can specialise minor in any field as well
the final degree will read tech in engineering science with specialisation minor engineering science options back to index to provide flexibility in curricula options have been created to pursue year tech in engineering science any student may opt for tech in engineering science post 1st year to pursue targeted focus area in science and engineering to meet demands of emerging industrial and academic requirements students will be required to complete core course requirements in the first two years of study where as they can outline their own curriculum in the form of electives for the remaining two years from courses across disciplines of engineering science and humanities the students will be awarded tech in engineering science similar to regular tech programme students can also use credits out of stipulated electives and additional credits to earn minor specialization as an add on to the tech degree year tech in engineering science students can pursue capability linked tech in engineering science to pursue newer structures of programmes in addition to classical programmes where represents the primary branch in which student has taken admissions after completion of the first year the student having cgpa of or more may opt to choose capability linked tech in engineering science students will be required to register for extra credits for the same the students pursuing this programme will take common courses and courses with concentration in engineering science in the 2nd year from the third year onwards the student would also start opting for courses related to the primary branch and elective courses in the area of both primary branch and engineering science at the end of the second year an option of primary branch change will also be available to meritorious students of this programme the students will be awarded tech in and engineering science tech in mechanical engineering and engineering science students of this programme would not be able to opt for any other specialization minor year sc in engineering science this option is designed mainly for under performing students as an exit plan the student would be required to complete required credits under institute and engineering science core and open electives categories domain options for tech
es back to index there are various combinations of complementary domains that student can opt in their 5th and 6th semester
few examples are cs ee cs bb cs chem cs math cs phy ai bb ai ee
cs chem back to index dancing molecular study of properties of different elements for substituting li ion batteries using molecular dynamics
molecular dynamics is computationally intensive task and required intense knowledge of mathematics chemical engineering and computer science
cse ee back to index electrical engineering deals with the study and the application of electricity electronics and electromagnetismcomputer engineering deals with the design of computer systems which involves the design of the new hardware or software where knowledge of electronics is prerequisite also we can clearly understand about the networking and the telecommunication
the objective of engineering science computer science and electrical engineering program is to design the systems and components that capture store process interpret and transmit information or signals
some of the most significant technological advances of the 20th century were either invented or put into practice by electrical engineers and computer scientists including electric power systems global broadcast and personal telecommunication systems computer systems computer networks medical instrumentation such as magnetic resonance imaging mri and computer aided tomography cat integrated circuits lasers household appliances and feedback control such as for autopilots
new technologies developed by electrical engineers and computer scientists are likely to be even more important in the 21st century as new era of intelligent information driven systems is made possible by fundamental advances in faster communication rates smaller devices and greater computational capabilities
ai ee back to index the fifth generation 5g wireless communications are expected to satisfy the diverse service requirements in various aspectsdue to the extreme range of 5g requirements the design and optimization of 5g networks becomes very challenging
the future 5g network will require robust intelligent algorithms to adapt network protocols and resource management for different services in different scenarios
artificial intelligence ai is feasible solution for the emerging complex communication system design
it is now appropriate to apply ai technology to 5g wireless communications to tackle optimized physical layer design complicated decision making network management and resource optimization tasks in such networks the objective of engineering science artificial intelligence and electrical engineering program is to design the systems and components that has provided great potential and space for optimization in the field of electrical engineering
this can not only bring about significant improvement not only with regards to the economic aspects but also in the safety and the actual control of the operation
ever since artificial intelligence reached stage where it could be used in various fields in order to make work simpler it has been used widely in various fields of life primarily in robotics and computer programming
recent studies and investigations have shown that artificial intelligence can also be viable option in the field of electrical engineering especially as solution to some longstanding power system problems where conventional methods experience some difficulty
mech electrical back to index electromechanics focuses on the interaction of electrical and mechanical systems as whole and how the two systems interact with each other
this process is especially prominent in systems such as those of dc or ac rotating electrical machines which can be designed and operated to generate power from mechanical process generator or used to power mechanical effect motor
electromechanical robotics is one of its applications
civil engineering ai back to index with skylines of major cities around the world peppered with iconic buildings of all shapes and sizes we can all agree that the boundaries and standards of design and engineering have been pushed beyond their limits
this is all thanks to the biggest game changer in the industry artificial intelligence in 3d building information modeling bim the construction process involves safety risks that can lead to actual accidents
ai provides the opportunity for more accurate data gathering from real world context models to help civil engineers identify potential hazards in the construction process also ai enabled cameras and networks can continuously monitor all areas of construction and allow engineers to assess equipment use track progress and analyze activities in real time which helps in the early detection of potential risk hazards
good example of the application of such technology lies in the popularity of indus ai
this san francisco tech company set ai supported cameras around construction sites to provide real time footage while collecting and evaluating data to give construction managers insights on things like the movement of materials and labor deployment at various areas of the site
this interactive dashboard also allows civil engineers to anticipate anything that may prove dangerous and make better decisions with regard to worker safety
material science ai back to index materials science or solid state physics is plagued by the curse of dimensionality
this means even for reasonable number of constituents of material the structure property relationships are determined by high dimensional parameter space
these relationships are not something trivial to be elucidated only by experiments or theory
this is precisely where the applications of ai or machine learning ml come into play
bio ai back to index ai is also making its presence felt in biotechnology where it has become integral to many aspects of drug discovery and development
by leveraging machine learning ai can manage disparate clinical trial datasets enable virtual screening and analyze vast amounts of data
besides reducing clinical trial costs ai can gain otherwise unobtainable insights and feed them back into the drug development process
ai technologies to serve the biotech industry are being developed by several companies
their services are rapidly becoming indispensable as older methods like classical statistical analysis or manual image scanning reach their practical limits
