undergraduate program bioscience bioengineering iit jodhpur
tech in bioengineering duration years introduction every life form beginning from microscopic bacteria to multicellular organisms can be considered as system that has been perfected over millennia by evolution
beginning from thermodynamically efficient cellular processes to the molecular motors driving the bacterial flagella each product of evolution is an optimized engineering design that can be translated into real life applications across disciplines
today interdisciplinary biology is well complemented by engineering domains including electronics computer science mechanics and materials engineering
computational biology for example permits solving biology problems using statistical artificial intelligence and machine learning approaches
extensive use of computational biology is essential in data driven approaches including genomics proteomics transcriptomics and metabolomics
this amalgamation of biology and computer science has transformed the landscape of the infectious and non infectious diseases research allowing the development of new diagnostic and therapeutic modalities
today disease diagnosis is much more specific and sensitive thanks to biosensors which have far reaching applications not only in healthcare but also in the environment energy and agriculture
some applications include in situ biosensors for human biochemical parameters environmental contaminants soil moisture soil physicochemical parameters etc
advances in biology have not only permitted the early diagnosis of disease but have also contributed abundantly to therapeutic interventions
it is now possible to intervene in diseases taking multi pronged approach including the development of new drugs production of recombinant therapeutics development of implantable sensors control systems and editing genetic defects
similarly synthetic biology and genome editing tools have enabled the development of industrially important organisms for biofuel production as well as environmental remediation
together these technologies have significantly contributed to extending the healthy lifespan of the human race and have shown promise towards food water and energy security
in other words bioengineering as discipline has evolved to great extent in last couple of decades and now demands different intellectual outlook and scientific understanding for its further study
therefore an undergraduate academic program in bioengineering should reflect this rapid evolution of the discipline underlying scientific and technological advancements and interdisciplinary cross talk so as to capture the mind of the next generation
the indian institute of technology jodhpur is committed to achieving excellence in shaping the young minds of tomorrow with unique blend of teaching techniques and hands on research based learning
the institute works towards the holistic development of students allowing them to be able to assume leadership positions in industry academia and entrepreneurial ventures
the department of bioscience bioengineering is committed to this goal and has developed academic programs that are interdisciplinary in nature and include substantial component of courses from other science and engineering departments
the department aspires to produce graduates who can develop solutions to tackle local and global problems related to healthcare energy environment water and food
objectives of the program the
program in bioengineering at iit jodhpur aims to equip our graduates with in depth knowledge and hands on training in areas that will be relevant for the next decade
the curriculum attempts to deliver theoretical and practical content that will be needed to address societal needs that will challenge our graduates globally as well as locally
this includes an unconventional and highly interdisciplinary approach towards problem solving
the key objectives of this program are to provide strong foundational understanding of the concepts in biology engineering mathematics and allied sciences enabling students to approach bioengineering problems through interdisciplinary lenses
ii to stimulate the development of disruptive technologies and futuristic research in the domains of healthcare energy and the environment
expected graduate attributes graduates of the
tech program in bioengineering will be able to choose design develop and implement engineering solutions to solve technical problems in bioscience and bioengineering using trans disciplinary knowledge and skill set
ii be able to think out of the box to solve critical challenges and be able to innovate novel technologies for affordable healthcare clean energy sustainable agriculture and safe environment
iii be able to work effectively as member and leader in multi disciplinary team
iv have the requisite skills and knowledge to be bio entrepreneurs
be able to effectively communicate biological bioengineering problems and solutions within the professional sphere and with the society at large
vi be committed to high ethical standards in professional and social practices
learning outcomes graduates of the
tech program in bioengineering will will have comprehensive understanding of the essential concepts of bioscience and bioengineering encompassing cell and molecular biology physiology immunology microbiology genetics genetic engineering multi omics computational and systems biology
ii will gain trans disciplinary knowledge and skillsets pertaining to biosensors bioimaging biomedical device development biomaterials engineering and drug discovery
iii will be able to design develop and implement technological solutions related to affordable healthcare clean energy sustainable agriculture and safe environment adopting trans disciplinary approach
iv will gain strong understanding of the fundamentals of basic science engineering designing computing and will get hands on training on cutting edge technologies
will be able to apply the knowledge of artificial intelligence and machine learning in solving complex biological problems
vi will learn to think critically to solve complex problems pertaining to bioscience and bioengineering
vii will develop communication skills verbal and written to communicate effectively in the professional and social sphere
viii will be committed to high ethical standards
ix will gain the knowledge and skills required to be an innovator entrepreneur
new skillsets targeted ability to design and develop ai based technologies in healthcare environment and agriculture
ii analyze the spatial complexity of living systems using imaging and image processing
iii analyze and solve complex problems in the diverse fields of bioscience and bioengineering using omics and systems biology
iv integrate multidisciplinary technologies for the development of lab on chip devices biosensors and other smart devices
integrate omics data to quantitative structure activity relationship for drug discovery and development vi design and develop smart biomaterials using micro and nano scale technologies vii design novel strategies for tissue engineering application viii design new therapeutic molecules in silico
tech in bioengineering with specialization duration years the students will have the opportunity to choose the specializations while pursuing their regular
the department is presently offering two specializations listed below
the department specializations are designed to enable students to cater to the emerging industrial and societal challenges in various domains of bioengineering
therapeutic engineering and drug discovery microbial systems engineering topic clouds view the list of topic clouds and mapping of the topic with the courses

programs for working professionals computer science engineering iit jodhpur
programs for working professionals introduction artificial intelligence ai is branch of computer science that aims to create machines to act with higher levels of intelligence and emulate the human capabilities of sense comprehend and act
the core problems of artificial intelligence include programming computers for certain traits such as knowledge reasoning problem solving perception learning and planning
ai technology development and applications are evolving rapidly with major implications for economies and societies
as the demand for such applications increases there is also an increasing need for building the future workforce for ai
from january the department of computer science and engineering iit jodhpur has started the executive tech
in ai program for working professionals
from this program transcends to its stackable avatar certificate diploma tech ai in line with the new education policy for working professionals
this program runs as combination of synchronous and asynchronous teaching
online lectures are offered on evenings or weekends for the convenience of working professionals
the minimum duration of this mode of tech
program is years and the credit requirement is the same as the regular tech in ai program at iit jodhpur
the major goal of this program is to train and enhance the skills of working professionals with strong foundations of artificial intelligence ai and state of the art ai tools and techniques and make them more industry competent
for developing the ai ecosystem this program will be executed in synergy with other tech programs running in iit jodhpur such as sensors iot cyber physical systems and advanced manufacturing and design
objectives this executive tech in ai program will offer students with deep knowledge of both fundamental ai technologies as well as application oriented ai
student completing this program will be capable to undertake careers in industry as well as academia
he she will have the option to explore variety of domains including manufacturing fintech healthcare agriculture food processing education retail customer engagement human and robot interaction intelligent automation smart city aid for differently abled accessibility technology
features of the program an initiative to cater to the stackable program ideology of the nep2020 for working professionals easy stackable option opportunities to exit or add on course to graduate to next stage
classes are conducted on the weekends or in the evening of the weekdays in online mode and students attend in synchronous audio visual mode
the credit requirement per semester is flexible
students can register for as many courses as they want satisfying the minimum and maximum credit requirements every semester program elective and open elective courses may be chosen from wide variety of courses offered across the departments and schools including computer science engineering electrical engineering school of aide mathematics mechanical engineering industry oriented courses such as ml ops and dl ops students can interact and ask questions during the lectures
recorded lectures and teaching materials are made available online
course management and discussions will be enabled via widely used tools such as google classroom piazza and moodle
there are mandatory two contact weeks in semester
students attend lectures and discussion sessions with instructors participate in offline evaluations and experience the iit environment in person
contact weeks may be organized in remote locations subject to higher demand
students can do their tech
projects on state of the art research topics across departments students may participate in projects funded by government funding agencies and industries
expected graduate attributes after completing this programme student will be able to develop an ability to comprehend fundamental concepts and hands on knowledge of the state of the art ai methodologies
design and build real world ai systems for complex planning decision making and learning solving application specific problems and to reason about them
conceive design and develop intelligent multi modal multi sensory man machine interfaces
design develop and deploy machine learning based applications using structured and unstructured data speech text images videos
understand and assess reliability dependability and trust worthiness of ai based systems
design and develop ai applications for resource constrained environments
adhere to evolving ethics and privacy laws across various domains and territories
plan and manage technical projects
learning outcomes understand the fundamentals of artificial intelligence machine learning inference engines speech vision natural language understanding robotics and human computer interaction
unify the knowledge of human cognition ai machine learning and data engineering for designing systems
demonstrate hands on knowledge of state of the art ai tools for real world problem solving
ability to develop real time and robust ai based systems with specific software hardware and data requirements
build solutions to explore fully immersive computer generated worlds in vr and overlay computer graphics onto our view of our immediate environment ar along with smart cognitive functionality
demonstrate advanced skills to comprehend and communicate effectively
carry out projects using intelligent cognitive solutions provided by ai algorithms to get more insights in stakeholder management risk modeling intelligent resource scheduling and managing project constraints with intelligent use of data models
program details includes stackable options from onwards course structure for certificate diploma and degree program categories certificate program requires minimum to credits and hours of contact hours
diploma requires minimum contact hours around credits certificate and diploma as part of the stackable mtech ai program the certificate and diploma can be obtained as an exit option from mtech ai the certificate and diploma can also be registered by the student individually
if the student follows the continuation cgpa requirements he she can stack up the credits to earn the next higher degree finally leading to mtech ai as well

research highlights chemistry iit jodhpur
research highlights designing phenalenyl based dinuclear ni ii complex an electrocatalyst with two single ni sites for oxygen evolution reaction oer
kamboj and metre inorg
chem new dinuclear ni ii complex ni2ii dtbh ply is synthesized from di tert butyl hydroxybenzylidene hydrazineyl 1h phenalen one dtbh plyh2 ligand and structurally characterized by various analytical tools including the single crystal ray diffraction scxrd technique
in the solid state both ni ii metal centers in complex exist in distorted square planar geometry and display the presence of rare ni anagostic interactions to form one dimensional linear motif in the supramolecular array
complex is further stabilized in the solid state by stacking interactions between the highly delocalized phenalenyl rings
the redox features of complex have been analyzed by the cyclic voltammetry cv technique in solution as well as in the solid state revealing the crucial involvement of both the ni ii metal centers for undergoing quasi reversible oxidation reactions on the application of an anodic sweep
complex modified glassy carbon electrode gc is employed as an electrocatalyst for oxygen evolution reaction oer in koh giving an oer onset at and very low oer overpotential mv vs the reversible hydrogen electrode rhe to reach ma cm current density
furthermore gc displayed fast oer kinetics with tafel slope of mv dec significantly lower tafel slope value than those of previously reported molecular ni ii catalysts
in situ electrochemical experiments and postoperational uv vis fourier transform infrared ft ir scanning electron microscopy energy dispersive ray spectroscopy sem eds and ray photoelectron spectroscopy xps studies were performed to analyze the stability of the molecular nature of complex and to gain reasonable insights into the true oer catalyst

office of research development iit jodhpur
welcome to the office of research development the office of provides specialized administrative and managerial support for the operation of sponsored research projects consultancy projects and other related activities of the institute
the office of facilitates interaction with national and international agencies supporting research development
also it promotes academia industry interactions and collaboration with larger laboratories and liaises between institute and funding agencies
further it supports faculty members in filing of patents and documenting ipr generated through research activity of the institute
the office of facilitates the following management of projects registration of sponsored consulting and institute projects raising invoice grants in aid bill receiving funds prepare utilization certificates appointment extension and resignations of project staff members payment of salary work related to fellowships project related purchases imports and their payments management of temporary advances to pis and project staff members settlement of advances payment for work assignments and preparation of cheques bank reconciliation accounts preparation management of investments
management of office preparation and publishing of booklets hard and soft copies related to office of newsletter institute research magazine developing maintaining database of contacts in industry academia and organisations in india and abroad designing and preparing flyers of different departments and laboratories of the institute and managing mous iprs corporate communications events and relationships
latest news live updates applications for international mobility research grant imrg are invited from the new faculty members who are yet to complete years from the date of joining at iit jodhpur
last date of submission of the imrg application is december
last date of submission of the imrg application is extended till january click here for details kb click here for application form no and travel within india from project fund as per the office order no
iitj reg dated 30th july it is mandatory to obtain prior approval from the dean of research and development dord through the head before undertaking any travel within india funded by the project

departments can design various sets of compulsory courses based on their research themes

degree can choose to pursue an additional tech
degree by fulfilling the tech
thesis work must be separate from the ph thesis
the additional degree will be awarded alongside the ph degree only upon the completion of the ph program

introduction to iit jodhpur indian institute of technology jodhpur iit jodhpur
introduction to iit jodhpur the tree of knowledge and technology at iit jodhpur campus indian institute of technology jodhpur was established in to foster technology education and research in india
the institute is committed to technological thought and action to benefit the economic development of the country
scholarship in teaching and learning scholarship in research and creative accomplishments and relevance to industry are the three driving forces at iit jodhpur
iit jodhpur functions from its sprawling residential permanent campus of acres on national highway north northwest of jodhpur towards nagaur
this campus is meticulously planned and envisioned to stand as symbol of academics simple yet profound
the institute is committed to multidisciplinary approach to technology development
hence it has established state of the art laboratories for basic research and has organised its academic degree programs through departments while coordinating research activities through centers for technologies
important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur

last updated mar pm
post graduate program chemistry iit jodhpur
post graduate program introduction the department of chemistry hosts two year sc
chemistry download file kb program in chemistry
the primary objective of sc
chemistry program at iit jodhpur is to bring out professionals with the fundamental knowledge and skill sets of various aspects of chemistry
the program comprises strong collection of courses spanning among organic inorganic physical and theoretical chemistry with associated laboratory and hands on sessions to provide practical knowledge
as part of the sc
program students carry out one major and one minor research project under the guidance of the faculty members of the department to get an exposure to the modern research going on in various subdomains of chemistry
objective of the programme the major objective of the program is to bring out professionals having fundamental knowledge and specific skill sets of various aspects of chemistry such as organic reactions and their mechanism main group and transition metals organometallics and catalysis physical and spectroscopic techniques inorganic and solid state material synthesis computational and theoretical chemistry
the department focuses on preparing future students to take up challenges at various levels of academics ds and industries
expected graduate attributes capability to appreciate synthetic methods for development of important chemical products such as pharmaceutical drugs paints petrochemicals food additives plastics etc ability to use characterization tools for analysing reactions and monitoring chemical processes for product development ability to handle analytical spectroscopic microscopic and other instrumental methods for interdisciplinary research areas use of computational tools to understand and analyse chemical and biophysical processes molecular structures and advanced materials ability to comprehend write and present scientific articles and technical reports develop oral and written communication skills appreciation and adherence to norms of professional ethics and sense of intellectual integrity ability to plan and manage technical research projects and work in team learning outcomes strong fundamentals in theoretical and practical aspects of organic inorganic and physical chemistry develop appreciation of hands on wet chemistry analytical instrumentation and experimental data handling skills to solve scientific problems to address technological challenges in various domains knowledge of computational and theoretical chemistry skills for material synthesis and processes for device integration develop the ability to exercise independent learning thinking and problem solving skills develop the ability to interact in cross cultural environment and acquire time and project management skills for carrying out guided research work in any of the research areas related to science and technology develop skills to communicate scientific learning and present ideas duration years proposed intake including ews eligibility jam chemistry qualified candidates will be considered as per the existing norms of sc
faculty coordinator prof ananya debnath email important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur

technical staff members physics iit jodhpur
technical staff members the department of physics has vacancies in regular staff members positions at the levels of junior technician technician junior technical superintendent technical superintendent
these staff members are expected to work closely with faculty members and students alongside other staff members to meet the science and technology objectives of the institute
interested persons are invited to apply online

last updated feb pm
electrical engineering iit jodhpur
welcome to the department of electrical engineering the department of electrical engineering primarily focuses on imparting quality education and preparing students to face future technological challenges
the vision of the department is to enhance the research environment and to innovate in pedagogy to address the challenges of socio economic and human resource development
the department is committed to engage in high quality research by faculty members and students and in the pursuit of excellence in teaching
the department offers tech
in electrical engineering with broad range of major and minor specializations and three state of the art tech
programs in sensors and internet of things siot cyber physical systems cps and intelligent communication systems ics
the department also runs dual degree tech
ph programs in intelligent communication systems sensors and internet of things siot and cyber physical systems cps as well as ph program with broad range of specializations covering detailed aspects of microelectronics signal processing communication microwave photonics power systems control systems etc
the department is also starting new executive mtech program in intelligent vlsi systems for working professionals from july
the prospective undergraduate and postgraduate students are encouraged to visit the academic programs ug program pg programs and doctoral programs page of the department to know more about the details of courses offered in the department
to know more about the current research themes of the faculty members the prospective students are encouraged to visit the research overview page of the department
technology tracks currently pursued by the department include emerging and challenging fields that culminate at the intersection of several traditional research areas
current technology tracks active collaboration is on going with organizations like freescale semiconductors global foundries aiims jodhpur dst drdo and isro to name few
the department has been attracting various sponsored projects from organizations since its inception

indian institute of technology jodhpur course booklet for tech
cse and dual degree tech
cse phd programs offered by the department of computer science and engineering july tech and tech ph dual degree programs in computer science and engineering introduction traditionally computer science and engineering cse teaching were focusing on two major areas theories and systems database computer hardware and operating systems
with the advent of the era of cloud computing edge computing ai and big data the discipline is being transformed by incorporation of new emerging technologies
it is becoming an instrumental tool in solving major problems faced by modern society such as energy natural disasters pollution and water
through this newly revamped tech and tech ph dual degree programs in cse iit jodhpur is making conscious effort to divulge from the traditional path and planning to reposition itself to become leading institute in this new genre of computer science education
objectives this tech and tech ph dual degree programs in cse will offer students with deep knowledge of core and applied computer science
through this programme student will learn niche subject areas which are of paramount importance in the modern big data era such as computer systems and security
this programme is aiming at imparting the necessary breadth and depth to the students for pursuing careers in academics as well as in industry
this programme is aiming at extending undergraduate computing skills with up to date and in depth expertise in specialized areas of computer systems and security
expected graduate attributes after completing this programme student will be able to develop an ability to
understand fundamental concepts and hands on knowledge of emerging fields in computer science
conceive design and develop state of the art scalable parallel and distributed systems
solve big data problems through the knowledge of advanced data structures distributed algorithmic design analysis and applications
design and develop network protocols for wireless sensor mobile and vehicular networks
ideate implement and integrate cryptographic fault tolerant algorithms for large scale distributed systems
understand state of the art hardware platforms for running compute intensive distributed algorithms
ability to understand and apply evolving ethics and privacy laws across various domains and territories
plan and manage technical projects learning outcome
understand the fundamentals of algorithmic complexity advanced computer architecture advanced network and security protocols
apply appropriate design principles framework and protocols to develop dependable systems
demonstrate hands on knowledge of cutting edge simulation synthesizing programming tools
ability to design and develop system architecture for mobile cloud fog and edge computing
demonstrate hands on knowledge of virtualization data center design and management and software defined networking
skills to comprehend and communicate effectively
apply appropriate project and business management principles and tools for real world problems
course structure for the tech

academic programmes rules regulations for student enrolled from july onwards indian institute of technology jodhpur
introduction academic programmes at indian institute of technology jodhpur are designed to develop the highest calibre human resource capable of understanding the new patterns of knowledge creation across disciplines obliterating traditional boundaries between science humanities social sciences and engineering
iit jodhpur aims to produce quality professionals who would be able to address profound and wide ranging societal challenges of the 21st century such as energy food water housing mobility and health
in addition to imparting scientific knowledge iit jodhpur endeavours to inculcate human qualities of courage integrity fairness humility and team effort among its graduates through curricular co curricular and extra curricular activities on campus
the undergraduate academic programmes focus on developing temper for the life long process of learning creative thinking and exploring
the postgraduate academic programmes focus on developing deep understanding of the subject of study coupled with creative inquisitiveness and the ability to address and solve new problems with free and objective mind
the academic programmes are based on three tenets of semester system credit system and relative grading
these academic programmes are administered by duly constituted academic committee ac through the office of academics
the ac will be chaired by dean academics
in the absence of dean academics associate dean academics pg associate dean academics ug will discharge the responsibilities of dean academics
the senate of the institute has formulated set of guidelines and rules to ensure high standard of performance as well as for smooth functioning of the academic programmes
within this broad set of guidelines subject to the approval of senate different programmes can include additional academic requirements as deemed necessary for that programme
these academic programmes are continuously monitored by the senate and make appropriate modifications improvements as and when necessary
academic session the academic session normally runs from the end of july in one year to the middle of july in the next year
the academic session is divided into three parts two regular semesters and summer term as per the following timelines semester from the fourth week of july to the last week of november semester ii from the last week of december to the last week of april summer term from the middle of may to the middle of july
excluding the days of the examinations the total number of days of instruction in semester is at least
academic calendar the academic calendar gives the exact dates of all important events during the academic session such as orientation registration the commencement of classes adding and dropping of courses submission of documents examinations submission of grades project evaluation declaration of results mid semester recess and vacation
this calendar is approved by the senate
registration students are required to register for prescribed credits as per the programme on the dates specified in the academic calendar
if the student does not register by the date of registration she he can register by paying the fine and register before the date of late registration
provisional registration fresh student joining the institute who is awaiting the final results of the qualifying examination is allowed to register provisionally on submission of certificate from her his last institute stating that she he has appeared in the final examination of the qualifying degree
the student is required to submit documents of having passed the qualifying examination by the last date given in the academic calendar for the registration to be regularized
late registration if the student does not register by the date of late registration as mentioned in the academic calendar he she will be deregistered
the appeal against deregistration may be made to chairman senate through dean academics
chairman senate may permit the continuation of registration on payment of an appropriate fine
adding and dropping of courses student may add or drop course with approval of the faculty advisor till the last date of registration
withdrawing from course after the last date of add drop student will have the option of withdrawing from course
the last date of course withdrawal will be typically within four weeks from the beginning of the semester as specified in the academic calendar
the withdrawn course will be mentioned in the student grade card with letter grade
cancellation of semester registration absence for period of four or more weeks during semester shall result in automatic cancellation of the registration of student from all the courses in that semester
summer course registration department may offer summer courses to enable the students to clear their backlog courses and or regular credit courses
course will run during summer provided faculty member is available for running the course
the course is offered on approval of the dean
teaching and evaluation teaching medium of instruction the medium of instruction is english
course structure all courses have associated credits
credits of course are based on the number of contact hours for lectures tutorials and practicals student on successful completion of the course with passing grade will earn an equivalent number of credits
based on the required academic maturity level of students for attending course course is assigned level
course can consist of independent components which can be completed independently
these components are called fractals
course is identified by unique number
following tables define all parameters related to course numbering scheme

and ph programmes an level course should have level course as pre requisite tech
cr ph cr approval of courses the senate approves each course along with its weight in terms of credits
only the approved courses may be offered during any semester summer term
list of courses the list of courses to be offered in program is finalized before the beginning of the semester by the department hosting that program
categories of courses courses offered can be from one of the following categories regular course regular course has hours of classroom engagement per one credit
regular course can be under elective or core compulsory categories
elective courses of or level will run if minimum of students registers for the course
or level elective course can run with minimum of registered students
course not running as regular course may be offered as self study course as per the following section
self study course self study course will be from the list of courses approved by senate
the main features of self study course are as follows student may be allowed to register for an elective course as self study course provided that the course is not running in that semester as regular course or student is not on campus during the course with prior approval of the competent authority
student may also be permitted to register for core or program compulsory course in self study mode provided he she has failed in it earlier and the course is not being offered as regular course during that semester or student is not on campus during the course with prior approval of the competent authority
students should apply for self study course with the appropriate recommendation of course coordinator and dugc drc irc
the final sanction of self study course to student is made by the dean academics
formal classroom engagement may not be there for self study course but laboratory design and computation exercises will be conducted if they form an integral part of the course
the course coordinator conducts the assessment as per the evaluation norms for the award of letter grade at the end of the semester
self study course will appear with the approved course title on the transcript
not exceeding of the total graded credit can be opted as self study course
independent study course independent study enables student to pursue course credit on an academic topic of interest under the supervision of faculty member
the term independent does not mean not involving an instructor but independent of regular class engagement
self disciplined and having sense of own direction and goals are fundamental requirements of an independent study course
student having cgpa more than or equal to can opt for independent study courses with the recommendation of supervising professor
the main features of an independent study course are as follows student may be permitted to register for topic of interest as an independent study course not exceeding credits in self study mode at most once during the program
such independent study course will be counted toward open elective requirements of the program and will appear with the title independent study on the transcript
students should apply for an independent study course with the appropriate recommendation of course coordinator and dugc drc irc
the final sanction of an independent study course to student is made by the dean academics
normally no formal lectures will be held for an independent study course but laboratory design and computation exercises will be conducted if they form an integral part of the course
the course will be arranged planned and managed by supervising professor
the course coordinator conducts the assessment as per the evaluation norms for the award of letter grade at the end of the semester
conduct of courses each course is conducted by the instructor with the assistance of the required number of tutors as needed
the instructor is responsible for conducting the course evaluating the performance of students awarding the grades at the end of the semester and transmitting the grades as per subsection
the student shall have access to his her answer paper of all the written examinations conducted for given course which may be shown to him her by the instructor concerned
the instructor must keep all evaluation records in his custody at least for the next six months
course approved by the senate of iit jodhpur can be conducted through either direct contact ii synchronized remote contact iii guided study mode or combination of these
in direct contact mode coordinator or instructor will have face to face physical interaction with the students
synchronized remote contact mode would require the faculty and or the students to be concurrently connected to the classroom interaction via electronic conferencing involving both audio and video communication
however there has to be direct interaction between faculty and students for limited hours typically of the stipulated contact hours
in guided study mode faculty would guide single or group of students to take up the study of an approved course via self learning and discussion oriented contact sessions
this mode can be also adopted for enabling student to take up study of new course not yet approved by the senate under the supervision of faculty
all formal evaluation component applicable to courses being run through any one of the above mentioned modes will be conducted under direct physical supervision of faculty
all laboratory component of course requiring the use of the specialized facility will be conducted through direct contact
teaching assignments the instructors and tutors for all courses to be offered during the semester are allocated by the head of the department hosting the program
when faculty members of other departments are also required to participate in teaching particular course the allocation shall be done in consultation with the head of that department
auditing of courses student may audit course in addition to the prescribed academic load requirement with the permission of dean academics on the recommendations of instructor of the course being audited and the faculty advisor
under this arrangement the course will be listed in the grade card of the student
attendance requirements student should have full attendance in each course
unless the student takes leave of absence for valid reasons the student has to attend every lecture tutorial or lab session
the attendance records must be made available to the student after every lecture
even if the student attendance falls below the student will be allowed to appear for the exams
students not meeting the attendance criterion of will be required to score at least grade to pass course
these students would be awarded grade if their marks are lower than the cut off for grade in course
evaluation the performance of students in course is evaluated continuously using their interaction in the classroom and performances in examinations the laboratory work if any and term papers and projects
the performance of students in course is evaluated through examinations and on continuous basis
the senate will decide from time to time on the system of tests and examinations in each subject in each semester
minor minor and major examinations are mandatory components of the evaluation of regular week long lecture course
the minor examinations shall be of minutes duration and the major examination of minutes
fractal courses less than credits must have at least one examination
the total weightage of examination component three examinations for week long course shall be between to of the total weightage of evaluation measure
evaluation policy has to be known from the first day of the class
the continuous evaluation may include but not limited to interaction in the classroom quizzes assignments tutorials laboratory work term papers and projects
grading system letter grades at the end of the semester student is awarded relative letter grade in each course by the instructor offering the course considering the performance of the student during the semester with respect to those of the other students registered in the course
it is proposed that ten regular letter grades namely and shall be awarded in each course
each letter grade is associated with numerical equivalent on point scale shown in table
in addition there are four special letter grades namely and which stand for incomplete satisfactory thesis continuation and unsatisfactory respectively
the faculty member shall upload grade on the portal as per the stipulated time schedule
table letter grade and its equivalent grade points
letter grade grade points comment exceptional outstanding excellent very good good average below average marginal poor fail incomplete satisfactory in course thesis continuation unsatisfactory withdrawn detailed description of some of the grades is given below
grades description grade has the same grade point as grade
the grade indicates exceptional performance and is awarded only in the course in which the number of registered students is more than
it should not exceed of the total strength of the particular theory or lab course
the grade is not awarded for projects seminars
grade will be awarded to student who has scored marks less than the cutoff marks for grade and has met the attendance criterion of the institute
students who obtain an grade will be eligible to appear in an additional examination
if they perform satisfactorily they become eligible for getting the grade converted to grade otherwise they will continue to have grade
the student will have only one chance to appear for the additional examination for an grade
the additional examination will be conducted within the first week of the next semester
the date of additional examination of institute core courses for undergraduate students centrally notified while for all other courses the date would be announced by the respective course coordinators
student can appear for maximum of three such additional tests in given semester
if student can not appear for the test due to any reason he she will not get any additional chance
if student with grade in course does not pass the course through the additional test or obtains an grade in the course he she has to repeat the course if it is core course
in case the course is elective the student may take the same course again or any other course
and grades are not counted in the calculation of the cgpa however these are taken into account in the calculation of the sgpa
an grade is temporarily awarded to student on his her request to denote incomplete performance in course on approval of instructor
an grade is awarded in case missing an evaluation component due to absence on medical grounds or other special circumstances
requests for grade should be made at the earliest but not later than the last day of major tests
all evaluation requirements for such students in the corresponding course should be completed before the end of the first week of the next semester
upon completion of all course requirements the grade is converted to regular grade
this grade is awarded to student with satisfactory performance in non graded and audit courses
if the stipulated requirements are not fulfilled the unsatisfactory grade is awarded
the grades obtained in an audit course are not considered in the calculation of sgpa or cgpa
this grade is awarded to student with satisfactory performance in thesis for continuation this grade is awarded to student with unsatisfactory performance in thesis audit or non graded courses
this grade is awarded to student who has opted to withdraw from course
withdrawal from course is permitted until the date specified in the academics calendar
procedure for declaration of results the following procedure would be followed for declaration of results and timeline would be as per academic calendar
evaluation scheme to be displayed by instructors at the start of semester
display of answer sheets and all scores by instructors
finalization of grades by instructors
grade moderation by department institute level committee for department institute wide courses
recommendation of grades by the head of the department after scrutiny of grades by moderation committee
display of grades by instructors
grade correction if any within hours of display of grades after approval of the head of the department
approval of grade by the dean ug pg
finalization of results by the dean ug pg
approval of results by the chairman senate
results once approved can not be changed under any circumstance
in exceptional cases the student may appeal to the chairman senate for reconsideration with formal justification
grade point averages semester grade point average the semester grade point average sgpa is weighted average of the grades earned by student in all the courses credited by her him and reflects her his academic performance in the respective semester
if the grade points associated with the letter grades earned by student in courses registered during semester are and the corresponding credits then the sgpa is given by the following formula the sgpa is calculated on the basis of grades obtained in all courses except audit courses and courses in which grade is awarded registered in particular semester
cumulative grade point average the cumulative grade point average cgpa indicates the overall academic performance of student in all the courses registered up to the latest completed semester
the cgpa is computed similarly as the sgpa considering all the courses in all semesters
the cgpa is calculated on the basis of all pass grades except audit courses and courses in which grade is awarded obtained in all completed semesters
the sgpa and cgpa calculation are based on credits earned completed at iit jodhpur alone
grade card and transcript grade card shall be issued to each student at the end of each semester and transcript at the end of the programme
iit jodhpur grade card and transcript will only indicate the courses credits and grades completed at iit jodhpur and the total number of credits without grades earned in other academic institutions in particular semester if applicable
waiver of requirements in special cases the rules and regulations procedures and requirements stated in this manual other than those related to admissions to the academic programmes may be waived in special circumstances by the ac on the recommendation of the department with the approval of chairman senate
all such exceptions have to be reported to the senate
make up examinations student who has missed minor or the major examination due to genuine reasons like illness etc
may be permitted to write make up examination for the missed components
the student should make an application to the dean academics through the course instructor within ten days from the date of the examination missed explaining the reasons for their absence
applications received after this period need not be entertained
the permission to take make up examination will be given under exceptional circumstances such as admission to hospital due to illness
student needs to produce the necessary document subject to one of the following categories students residing in the hostels should produce medical certificate issued by the medical officer of the institute that he she was admitted to the hospital during the period of the missed quiz exam
students residing in the hostels and taking medical consultation with outside doctors are required to obtain written permission from the medical officer of the institute hospital before they proceed for such consultation
students residing in the hostels but taking medical consultation with outside doctors are required to obtain an endorsement on the certificate of treatment by the medical officer of the institute hospital
student staying outside the campus permanently temporarily must produce medical certificate from the registered medical practitioner and the same should be duly endorsed by the parent guardian
slot wise make up examination for major will be held during the makeup supplementary week as per academic calendar of the subsequent semester for those who are permitted to take the make up examination
student who misses this make up examination will not normally be given another make up examination
however in exceptional cases of prolonged illness resulting in the student missing make up examination the chairman of the senate in consultation with the dean of academics may permit the student to appear for make up examination any day before the end of the first week of the next semester
under no circumstances prorating can be done for the missed examinations or attendance
regulations for undergraduate programmes
undergraduate programmes iit jodhpur currently offers bachelor of technology
programs and capability linked opportunities for undergraduate students to complete minor interdisciplinary area specializations or department area specialization
student can also opt for tech
dual degree in departmental area or minor in entrepreneurship leading to tech
in entrepreneurship or department area specialization in engineering innovation
undergraduate admissions tech
programmes general admissions admissions to the tech
programmes are made once year in july through the all india level joint entrance examination jee conducted by iits
the procedures and other requirements for admission are specified in the jee information brochure brought out every year
cultural exchange programmes admissions are also offered to foreign nationals under the cultural exchange fellowship program of the government of india administered by indian council of cultural relations iccr new delhi
persons interested in these programs are required to apply through the indian high commission embassy in their respective countries
change enhancement of program student already admitted to tech
programme may switch to another department through branch change
student can enhance his her programme to minor interdisciplinary area specialization leading to tech
in minor interdisciplinary area major department area specialization leading to tech
in department area minor in entrepreneurship minor in entrepreneurship leading to tech
in entrepreneurship and department area specialization in engineering innovation through industrial translation
the rules governing these changes are given in section branch change and section for all others
reservation policy the reservation policy of government of india will be followed
student exchange program student exchange program provides for one two semester visit by students between the two academic institutions on reciprocal basis
the visiting student must be full time student enrolled in degree program in their home institution
the exchange program is open to full time undergraduate and graduate students of each home institution
it will be executed as per the pre approved arrangement including an mou between the institutions
selection of students under such program will be made by the dugc after assessing the preparedness of students to meet the requirements of the planned coursework research at the host institution and the relevance of the planned coursework research to the enrolled degree program
visiting student may carry out research or take courses for credit or otherwise or may use other academic facilities as per pre approved arrangement including an mou
under exchange program student of iit jodhpur can register at another academic institution in india or abroad with the expectation of credit transfer up to credits per semester in lieu of the course work or research work through pre approved arrangement including an mou
on return the student may apply to dugc for waiver of credits from her his program requirements which he thinks are equivalent to the courses successfully completed at the visited academic institution as an exchange student
with the application the student must submit an official transcript of the grades obtained by her him at the visited academic institution as an exchange student and other documents material that the concerned dugc may require for evaluation
the dugc will determine by whatever means it deems fit the equivalent credits requirements for which the student may be given waiver in her his programme at iit jodhpur
dugc will submit its recommendations to course equivalence committee for its approval
moreover the credits grades indicated in the grade sheet obtained from the academic institution in which the student has completed the courses should be used by the student as part of his her transcripts
iit jodhpur transcripts will only indicate the courses credits and grades completed at iit jodhpur and the total number of credits without grades earned in other academic institutions in particular semester
the cgpa calculation based on credits earned completed at iit jodhpur alone is to be considered for award of prizes
the credits earned at the visited academic institutions will be taken into account for the calculation of minimum required credits for the award of the degree
visiting students visiting student is student who is registered for degree in recognized institute or university in india or abroad and who is officially permitted by that institute or university to complete part of his her academic requirements at iit jodhpur
for that purpose the visiting student may carry out research or take courses for credit or otherwise or may use other academic facilities
person can be admitted as visiting student on duly sponsored application to the dean academics
visiting student may be admitted for maximum period of one year
visiting student will be required to pay all applicable fees depending upon the status program and nationality
students so admitted also will be governed by all rules regulations and discipline applicable to regular students of the institute
cancellation of admission students admitted provisionally or otherwise to any program shall submit copies of their mark sheets provisional certificates etc
of the qualifying examination and other documents by the last date specified for the purpose in the academic calendar
the senate reserves the right to cancel the admission of any student who fails to submit the prescribed documents by the specified date or to meet other stipulated requirement
also the senate reserves the right to cancel the admission at any later time if it is found that the student had provided some false information or suppressed relevant information while seeking admission
fulfillment of admission requirements admission to any undergraduate program requires that the applicant be eligible go through the laid down admission procedure and pay the prescribed fees
the senate should formally approve all admissions to the undergraduate programmes
curriculum and academic requirements academic load calculation of credits for course for ug programmes the total credits for course are calculated by taking into account the number of lectures tutorial and practical hours assigned as given in table
each credit is associated with the number of contact hours and the expected number of self learning hours on an average to be spent by the student for each contact hour to meet academic demand of the course
table ltp structure and credit assignment
type distribution of contact and beyond contact hours total credits tc th contact hours ch beyond contact hours bch total hours th hour of lecture hour of tutorial hour of lab hour of project contact hour for project refers to the involvement of students in the laboratory discussion etc
capability linked opportunities for undergraduate students tech
student can opt for one of the following capabilities linked opportunities major department specialization interdisciplinary specialization minor tech
dual degree eligibility criteria for opting major department specialization minor interdisciplinary specialization student should have earned minimum credits with the cgpa more than or equal to at the end on th semester to opt for capability linked opportunity
dual degree in the chosen specialization students who have already opted for major department specialization minor interdisciplinary specialization can opt for dual degree programme from the eighth semester provided they have earned minimum credits with at least cgpa at the end of the 7th semester
minimum credit requirements major department specialization interdisciplinary specialization and minor student needs to complete total of credits in the major department specialization or minor interdisciplinary specialization area offered
credits out of can be taken up as part of open electives whereas the student is required to opt for an additional credits in the form of course overload
this will be indicated on the degree
registration for additional courses for major department specialization interdisciplinary specialization and minor starting from the sixth semester students having earned credits with cgpa more than or equal to are permitted to take up to extra credits as part of their normal load which forms part of her his additional learning component every semester in addition to the prescribed courses for their degree
dual degree in the chosen specialization student needs to complete total of credits in the minor department or interdisciplinary area offered
it will include credits earned for major department or minor interdisciplinary area specialization excluding the project credits

academic performance requirements the minimum requirements on credits earned for continuation in
programme at the end of the second registered semester is specified in the following table table criteria for continuation at the end of the second registered semester description minimum number of credits to be earned gen obc gen ews sc st pd minimum for continuation if student does not meet the criteria specified in table he she may opt to restart the programme or else their registration will be terminated

dean ug pg should propose guideline for mentoring the student under probation
dean of students should also involve in the process
if student chooses to restart after the first two registered semesters then the credits earned and semesters registered till that point will not be counted towards the graduation requirements
the re start will be indicated on the transcript
criteria to put student on academic probation from the third semester onward if student fails to earn minimum of credits in any registered semester then he she will be placed on academic probation
student placed on probation shall be monitored including mandatory attendance in classes special tutorials and mentoring
mentoring would comprise structured guidance under senior student
if student is on academic probation for two consecutive registered semesters then his her registration will be terminated
if student earns less than or equal to credits in any registered semester warning shall be given to him her and intimation be sent to the parents
the two semesters allowed for the academic probation should be counted towards the total duration allowed for the program
assistantship for tech
dual degree programmes the students of dual degree programmes will be considered for award of institute research teaching assistantship if they have earned credits by the end of the eighth semester
only those students who have either qualified gate or have cgpa more than will be eligible for this assistantship
the assistantship will be provided for maximum period of months beginning from the summer semester following the eighth semester provided the student is registered for tech
major project in that semester
student availing assistantship will be required to provide hours of assistance per week besides his her normal academic work
for continuation of assistantship student will need to secure sgpa of
student receiving assistantship will be eligible for total of days leave during the month period
he she will not be entitled to mid semester breaks winter and summer vacations
ug registration summer courses students can register for courses during summer up to maximum of eight credits on payment of registration fee at prescribed rates
the summer term is not regular semester and only academically deficient students who have low cgpa and or fewer credits than advised by the curriculum till that point in the programme major students minor students and such students who are short of maximum eight credits to complete all graduation requirements may register during this period
for core courses only students with failed backlog may be considered for summer registration
in case any vacancies are left in the courses being offered during the summer term once the requirements of the above categories of students are fulfilled other students may register for these courses to fulfil some of their graduation requirements in advance
the total number of contact hours for the courses remains the same as that during the regular semesters and therefore the courses run at an accelerated pace
the evaluation and grading patterns also remain the same as during the regular semesters
limits on registration an undergraduate student should register for minimum of credits in semester
the maximum number of credits permitted for ug student in semester is
an undergraduate student may be permitted to register for maximum of four credits per semester for independent study or self study course exceptions to regular rules regarding academic load under load students who are identified as academically deficient on academic probation may register for maximum of credits
over load overloading may be allowed only for students who are not on academic probation and satisfy one of the following criteria students are allowed to overload only when they need or less additional credits to complete all the requirements of their programme
beyond the second semester any student with cgpa of or higher may be allowed to overload
