admission to ph
program regular candidates admitted in this category shall not be registered student in any other academic program in india or abroad and shall not be full time or part time employee of any organization in india or abroad
the applicant must have master degree in engineering pharmacy agricultural science science humanities social sciences management with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen gen ews obc for sc st or the applicant must have mbbs with md ms bvsc with mvsc bds with mds bpth with mpth both with moth or equivalent with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen gen ews obc for sc st in bachelor degree or the applicant must have bachelor degree of minimum four year duration in engineering or science or medicine or pharmacy or agricultural science or veterinary science or equivalent with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen obc for sc st
admission under any special scheme like quality improvement programme qip defence personnel the procedures and criteria for admission of these candidates can be decided with the approval of chairman senate
admission of indian nationals residing abroad inra and foreign nationals inra candidates must have been residing abroad continuously for at least one year at the time of applying for admission
the applications of inra and foreign nationals may be processed by the departments as and when they are received or according to any schedule convenient to the department
the applications should be scrutinized to make sure that both in terms of qualifications and attainment they are comparable with the candidates admitted in the general category
department will conduct vc based interview for ascertaining suitability of the candidate
the applications of foreign nationals who are sponsored by the indian council of cultural relations iccr will be scrutinized by the department concerned to assess their suitability for admission to the programme
the department recommendation will be sent to the chairperson senate through the chairperson ac
admission to ph sponsored external part time candidates admitted in this category shall not be registered student in any other academic program in india or abroad
the applicant must have master degree in engineering pharmacy agricultural science science humanities social sciences management with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen gen ews obc for sc st or the applicant must have mbbs with md ms bvsc with mvsc bds with mds bpth with mpth both with moth or equivalent with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen gen ews obc for sc st in bachelor degree or the applicant must have bachelor degree of minimum four year duration in engineering or science or medicine or pharmacy or agricultural science or veterinary science or equivalent with at least marks or at least cpi or cgpa for gen obc for sc st
the applicant must have minimum of two years of work experience after qualifying degree in industry laboratories academic institutions at the time of registration to the program
the applicant must qualify the written test and or interview conducted by iit jodhpur
admission of non degree students non degree student is student who is registered for degree in recognized institute or university in india or abroad and who is officially sponsored by that institute or university to complete part of his her academic requirements at iit jodhpur
for that purpose the non degree student may carry out research or take courses for credit or otherwise or may use other academic facilities
any candidate can be admitted as casual student for specific courses provided the candidate satisfies the minimum eligibility criteria specified in section
the application must be routed through the office of dean
students so admitted will be governed by all rules regulations and discipline of the institute
reservation policy the reservation policy of government of india will be followed

leave rules application for leave of absence should be addressed to the convener drc and submitted with medical certificate if applicable
usually leave must not be availed of without prior approval of the drc
leave maximum of days in an academic year including leave on medical grounds mid semester break
the student must apply for leave in advance even during the summer winter term or mid semester break
phd student can not avail leave for more than days at stretch an tech sc
student can not avail leave for more than days at stretch the leave beyond days in an academic year may be granted in exceptional cases by drc without assistantship during that period
other leaves maternity leave for ph students is sanctioned as per the directives of mhrd
it will not be counted towards the maximum permissible duration of the programme
any other leave may also be permissible as per the guidelines of mhrd
semester withdrawal may be applicable in unforeseen circumstances or for medical reasons
absence for period of four or more weeks in any semester will result in automatic withdrawal from that semester
in all such cases the fellowship will be stopped with immediate effect and it will be continued for only after the student rejoins the institute
drc can approve and intimate office of academics regarding the leave of the student for records
in case the leave extends beyond semester it can only be approved through the academic committee
semester withdrawal on the medical ground will not be counted towards the maximum permissible duration of the programme for the student
academic leave academic leave can be requested for any of the following reasons such as
library work because of specialized collection at particular library
experimental work training using specialized facility not available at iitj
attending conferences workshops summer winter schools symposium
collaborative work as part of joint tech ph
collaborative work for specific problem related to his ongoing ph programme as indicated in his research proposal
exchange program as part of an exchange program with sister institution in india or abroad all such requests must be evaluated and approved by src drc irc before the leave is granted
src drc irc must also make recommendations regarding continuation of fellowship during academic leave
student may be allowed to spend maximum duration of one year on academic leave
student during academic leave can earn maximum of credits per semester as certified by src and approved by drc
financial assistance financial assistantship scholarship will be provided to the eligible students admitted under regular full time program
the eligibility criteria are as per the following sc
program merit cum means fellowship as per institute norms
dual degree program student having completed minimum credit requirements of an sc
program at the end of the second year with cgpa or have qualified gate can have financial support from mhrd or project fund
program regular tech
student with gate can have financial support from mhrd or project fund
student may be exempted from gate as per mhrd guidelines
executive tech program students admitted in this category will not be provided any financial assistance from the institute
ph dual degree program rules and regulations governing financial assistance for dual degree students will be at par with regular ph students
program regular ph student admitted to the institute will be eligible for financial assistance as per the norms of iit jodhpur
candidate having tech
four year program master degree with gate net jrf nbhm or equivalent can have financial assistance as per mhrd norms or equivalent applicable for non science departments
the requirement of gate is exempted for an applicant with tech or equivalent post bachelor degree student may be exempted from gate as per mhrd guidelines
ph student would be eligible for enhancement in the scholarship after two years provided the student has met qualifier requirement
the enhanced fellowship will commence from the date of completion of both the conditions
no arrears will be provided due to delay in completing the qualifier requirement
the teaching research assistantship of ph student shall stop from the day of submission of the thesis
rules and regulation for conduct and evaluation of tech sc
project allotment of students to supervisors will be made based on the preferences of the students and supervisor
the supervisor and area identification must be completed by the end of the first semester
program would advise until the student is assigned to regular supervisor
each semester letter grade will be awarded after examination by committee consisting of drc supervisor and two members conversant with the field of research with at least one member external to the department maybe outside the institute also
drc will appoint examiners in consultation with the supervisor
the same committee may examine more than one thesis
external member can be on vc
drc will ensure that award of letter grade would be as per relative grading policy
regular full time student must register for tech project part in the summer semester following nd semester
he must obtain satisfactory grade to continue tech project part in the next semester
his performance in the summer semester will be evaluated by his supervisor
rules and regulations for ph
dual degree program thesis supervisor selection of ph
candidates the selection should be based on merit and capability of the candidate to conduct research
there should at least be faculty member available or likely to join in the areas of interest of the candidate at the time of selection
appointment of thesis supervisor ph
students shall be admitted in the department and no supervisor shall be assigned at the time of selection
allotment of students to supervisor will be made based on the preferences of the students and supervisor
for both ph
dual degree supervisor and area identification must be completed by the end of the first semester the convener drc department research committee coordinator interdisciplinary research program would advise until the student is assigned to regular supervisor
there shall not be more than two supervisors in general for student
additional supervisor can be allowed with the approval of chairman senate
supervisor from outside the institute before the qualifier may be appointed on the request of student supervisor with the recommendation of the drc irc and the dean academics and approval of the chairman senate
the external supervisor will be requested to sign intellectual property right ipr and non disclosure agreement nda documents as part of acceptance
from the financial point of view the institute will have no obligation to the external supervisor
caretaker supervisor additional supervisor whenever the supervisor proceeds on short leave for period up to one year the drc irc interdisciplinary research committee shall appoint caretaker supervisor in consultation with the supervisor and the student to take care of the administrative requirements
whenever the supervisor proceeds on long leave for period more than year then an additional supervisor needs to be appointed on the suggestion of the supervisor
the supervisor going on lien or sabbatical may continue supervising students and the supervisor can opt for either an additional supervisor or caretaker supervisor
the supervisor on his her retirement or on resignation may continue as supervisor and will have to opt for an additional supervisor provided the student has cleared qualifier before the supervisor retirement resignation
composition of departmental research committee drc the drc will consist of coordinators of tech tech ph dual degree and ph programs of the department head of the department and senior professor associate professor proposed by the head
the drc will be chaired by professor associate professor nominated by head of the department
the chairman of the drc will be member of the academic committee
the drc has to be approved by dean academics
ph program coordinator will be the convenor of drc and will be proposed by the head
the tenure for the drc will be years
composition of interdisciplinary research committee irc the irc will consist of all coordinators of idrps and senior professor associate professor as the chairman of the irc will be nominated by dean academics
the chairman of irc will be member of ac
one of the idrp co ordinators would be convenor of the committee
the tenure for the irc will be years
the irc will be approved by the dean academics
change of research supervisor change of supervisor under exceptional circumstances shall be permitted by dean academics on the recommendation of the drc irc after obtaining the consent of the student ii the present supervisor and iii the proposed supervisor
if required realignment of student research program will happen under the supervision of the drc irc
student research committee src composition of src the src will normally consist of supervisor and three other faculty members conversant with the field of research with at least one member external to the department maybe outside the institute also
student research committee src for the candidate shall be proposed by the supervisor recommended by the drc irc and approved by dean academics
the src shall have one member identified as chairman for administrative functions and the supervisor will act as convener
chairman src is to be appointed by dean academics
the appointment of src should be completed by the end of the first semester
any subsequent changes in the composition of src shall be communicated through the drc irc to dean academics for record
chairman and supervisor shall be the internal examiners for the evaluation of the thesis
the src is expected to monitor the progress of the candidate until the completion of the program
annual progress monitoring meeting apm the academic progress of all registered ph students will be reviewed once in year by the src and peers in the institute
the review will be based upon annual progress report and ii poster presentation open to all
src will award satisfactory non satisfactory grade based upon and ii
two consecutive non satisfactory grades will lead to termination of registration
for implementational convenience academic calendar must indicate two days in year for conducting annual progress evaluation of research scholars
following the groupings of students and scheduling guidelines are suggested
for students joining in 1st semester of ay during 1st july to 30th september of the year
for students joining in 2nd semester of ay during 1st december to 28th february of the year
the first apm will be conducted after the successful completion of qualifier requirements
each semester satisfactory progress or unsatisfactory progress will be reported by the supervisor through the src for record
any situation in exception to the above will be processed by the academic committee and approved by dean academics qualifier requirements of ph student the following four components together will constitute qualifier candidacy requirements for ph
student and for ph
candidate to be eligible for the enhanced srf fellowship
completed course requirements with the minimum cgpa specified in section
cleared comprehensive examination
successful presentation on state of the art of the chosen field of research
successful presentation and defense of research proposal the ph
students are normally expected to complete successfully the qualifier requirements before the beginning of the fifth semester of his her registration in the ph
program and in any case not later than the beginning of the sixth semester after their registration in the ph
part time candidates will need to complete the qualifier requirement before the beginning of the seventh semester
comprehensive examination of ph
tech ph student the comprehensive examination must be designed to test the general capability of the student and the breadth of his her knowledge in the discipline and areas related to the field of research
the comprehensive examination shall be conducted by comprehensive examination committee of the department idrp
the comprehensive examination will consist of written test with three papers
departments will offer at least papers for conducting comprehensive examination
each paper will have broad coverage of one essential sub area of the department
the course content of these papers will be approved by the academic committee and the senate
the syllabus of papers for the comprehensive examination will not be the same as courses offered by the department it must be designed to test the breadth of the knowledge in that area
the src will specify set three papers as comprehensive requirement for particular student
these three papers may be selected from across the departments
the comprehensive examination requirements shall be intimated to the ph student within two weeks of src formation
all comprehensive examinations will be scheduled on two fixed days in year
it can coincide with the annual progress evaluation day of doctoral candidates
students may decide to appear for three papers on the same day or on different days
student can appear in the comprehensive examination before he she has completed the course requirements and satisfied the minimum specified cgpa requirement
if the performance of the ph student in comprehensive examination paper in the first attempt is not satisfactory he she will be given one more opportunity to appear within six months of the first attempt
for clearing of the comprehensive examination student must perform satisfactorily in all papers
comprehensive examination to be completed by the students ph within semesters direct ph and mtech phd dual degree within semesters state of art seminar sota every ph student is required to give general seminar in the department covering the state of art of the area of research
the presentation must also include patent landscaping of the area
the presentation will be evaluated by the src
candidate must have satisfactory grade in sota awarded by src for becoming eligible to present his research proposal
research proposal research proposal report presentation will be evaluated by src after successful completion of sota
the research proposal must include representative research work equivalent to research work worth credits
src will allocate letter grade based on the performance of the candidate
candidacy for the ph
degree student enrolled in the ph program is formally admitted to the candidacy for the ph degree after he she has completed the qualifier requirements for the degree
only such students who are admitted to the candidacy will be allowed to submit ph thesis
relaxation in tech
ph dual degree program tech ph dual degree students who have successfully completed all qualifier requirements under exceptional conditions as approved by src after years can also convert into part time external ph student and exit with tech degree if all conditions for part time external conversion are satisfied
the renewal of their registration for every semester however will be considered only if src finds their progress to be satisfactory and recommends the continuance of registration
cancellation of registration the registration of student whose progress is not found to be satisfactory by the src or who has not enrolled will be liable for cancellation
the registration of student who has not submitted his her thesis before the end of the maximum permissible period specified in section will be liable for cancellation
synopsis synopsis will consist of an open seminar and comprehensive internal closed door assessment of the research work through pre synopsis report by src
the student can submit the synopsis only if the src is satisfied with the quality and quantity of the work for submission as ph thesis
publications arising out of the thesis are to be submitted along with the pre synopsis report
students should submit the synopsis and thesis within six months of the open seminar date
if required an extension of three months may be permitted by dean academics on the recommendation of src
if the synopsis and thesis are not submitted in the specified period the student will be asked to present the synopsis again
on satisfactory completion of the prescribed courses with minimum cgpa specified in the comprehensive examination the sota the research work and the synopsis the student shall submit the requisite copies of the synopsis of his her research work in the required format through the supervisor and hod to the academic section for consideration of the src
the student should have at least one paper either published or accepted for publication in refereed journal refereed conference src members are required to certify the quality of journals conference
thesis submission and evaluation the supervisor shall propose list of examiners consisting of at least four faculty members from abroad and faculty members from india who have the expertise in the area in which the student undertook research
for selecting an examiner the following guidelines should be followed the examiners shall have at least years of post ph experience no two proposed examiners shall be from the same organization and the examiners can be faculty members or scientists of standing and repute
chairman senate may ask for additional names for examiners
the chairman senate shall select minimum two examiners from the proposed list of which at least one should be an examiner from outside india
the consent of these examiners shall be taken before sending the thesis to them
in addition src and supervisor shall be the examiners of the thesis
the viva voce will be conducted in the presence of src supervisor and the external examiner
one of the external examiners shall attend the thesis defense
the examiners shall submit written report on the thesis within period of two months from the date of receipt of the thesis
if both examiners declare the thesis as acceptable for award of ph degree the dean academics shall request the supervisor to hold the thesis defense by the student
if any of the examiners reject the thesis for the award of the ph degree the dean academics shall report the matter to the chairman senate for further consideration
the chairman senate will refer the thesis to third examiner under special circumstances else the registration of the student shall stand cancelled in the ph program
if both examiners reject the thesis for the award of the ph degree no degree shall be awarded by the institute and the registration of the student shall stand cancelled in the ph program
if an examiner suggests revision of the thesis the student shall resubmit the thesis after the revision within prescribed time period of months in consultation with the supervisor
when both examiners recommend the thesis as accepted for the student to defend the thesis or after the resubmission of the thesis after incorporating the suggestions corrections given by the examiners the student shall publicly present the thesis and defend the contention of the thesis
the committee consisting of chairman src supervisor the examiner from within india and other faculty members quorum would be chairman src supervisor and an external examiner who participated in the comprehensive examination of the student shall evaluate the thesis defense of the ph student also
on their recommendation dean academics shall recommend to senate to consider the award of ph degree to the student
graduation requirements student is deemed to have completed the requirements for graduation if she he has met the requirements laid down in clauses to below
minimum residential requirement table minimum residential requirement no
program qualifications at the time of joining the program minimum duration in semesters of residence

no dues clearance degree will be awarded after obtaining no dues clearance from all concerned sections as deemed necessary by dean academics no case of disciplinary action the student shall not be considered to have completed the academic requirements if there is any case of indiscipline pending against her him
award of degrees provisional certificate student who completes all the graduation requirements specified in clauses to can be provided with provisional certificate by the office of dean academics on the approval of chairman senate
degree certificate student who completes all the graduation requirements specified in clauses to is recommended by the senate to the board of governors bog for the award of the appropriate degree in the ensuing convocation
the degree can be awarded only after the bog accords its approval

anti sexual harassment policy iit jodhpur
anti sexual harassment policy iit jodhpur constituted internal complaints committee vide notification no iitj admin dated november in accordance with the provisions contained in sexual harassment of women at workplace prevention prohibition and redressal act
the terms of reference of the committee shall be as follows to organise regular awareness programs in the institute regarding sexual harassment for various stakeholders of the institute particularly women to recommend to the institute from time to time measures that should be taken to deter sexual harassment and acts that affect dignity of all persons in the campus to deal with such other matters and issues as may be considered connected with sexual harassment to anyone affecting their dignity and to advice the institute from time to time in matters of sexual harassment and also for upholding the dignity of everyone attached with the institute

engineering science iit jodhpur
welcome to the engineering science message from chairperson es undergratuate commitee engineering applications generally require multidisciplinary knowledge spanning across various disciplines of engineering and sciences
knowledge in humanities along with engineering and or sciences are also finding important applications
in engineering sciences es is unique bachelors degree program of iit jodhpur which aim to impart core knowledge in two targeted focus areas in science and engineering to meet demands of emerging industrial and academic requirements
any student admitted to iit jodhpur can opt for tech
in engineering science post 1st year
students will be required to complete core courses of es in the first two years of study whereas they can outline their own curriculum in the guidance of faculty advisor in the form of electives for the remaining two years from courses across disciplines of engineering science and humanities
in line with regular tech
programs students can also use credits out of stipulated electives and additional credits to earn minor specialization as an add on to the tech
the flexibility of curriculum selection as per students capabilities and interest with faculty guidance aims to impartsufficient knowledge to the students to be future ready professionals
institute background and vision innovations scientific discoveries and new technologies are the essential foundations of the indian institute of technology jodhpur
the institute focuseson building solid and in depth knowledge in the engineering domains and strongly encourages interdisciplinary learning
the courses offered are defined such that it enhances the imagination and creativity of the students to identify and solve new problems in society
our endeavour is to build an atmosphere for uninhibited ideas the institute offers tech programs computer science engineering electrical engineering engineering sciences artificial intelligence and data science mechanical engineering bioengineering materials engineering chemical engineering and civil and infrastructure engineering the institute also empowers students by allowing them to avail specializations minors and elective courses
program background engineering science is unique branch of engineering designed to cater to the upcoming demand of the industry and research training the best of students to have strong foundation in every field along with specialization in their topic of interest
our institute is one of the primary institutes offering an engineering science program
this programme is four year course
the first two years are devoted to topics which we believe all engineering undergraduates should study
in the third and fourth years the students have the opportunity to study any two complementary disciplines by drafting their own course structure
this fosters multi disciplinary thinking and integrated application of engineering scientific and mathematical principles in young students
the students can specialise minor in any field as well

physics iit jodhpur
welcome to the department of physics the subject of physics is inherently natural science subject based on strong theoretical foundations and experimental findings
the subject closely intersects with almost all the branches of engineering and the current research activities of physics across the world made huge impact on new advanced and emerging technologies
keeping these facts into consideration our department is committed to imparting high quality education based on strong academic curriculum comprising elements of theoretical concepts laboratory experiments and most importantly hands on experiments
the graduates of the department are expected to have deep insight into the domain of physics together with strong bent of mind to pursue cutting edge research and translate it into technology
we offer broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs
our year bs in physics with specialization is technology oriented science program and offers specialization in physics based emerging technology domains
the program also has the flexibility to convert into year bs tech
dual degree course
at the postgraduate level we have four degree programs viz
years sc tech dual degree in physics and materials engineering
the dual degree program also has additional two specializations namely functional materials and computational materials engineering
finally our ph program empowers scholars to pursue research in various domains
research areas the department has very good blend of highly qualified young and experienced faculty members in the areas of advanced physics
the department is also equipped with number of thematic laboratories which enable the students to carry out high end and impactful theoretical and applied research in the following focus areas research areas high energy physics and astrophysics theoretical research in domains flavour physics astrophysics particle physics cosmology and dark matter and dark energy
experimental research in flavor physics with the belle ii experiment
quantum physics open quantum systems theoretical and experimental aspects of quantum optics quantum information quantum field theory quantum statistical mechanics and quantum cosmology
condensed matter and plasma physics group experimental and theoretical research on molecular electronics organic electronics cold plasma technologies and applications energy storage solar cells light emitting diodes solar water splitting soft matters statistical physics computational physics multiscale modelling phase ordering dynamics glasses amorphous solids polymers and solid electrolyte materials physics of living matter active matter physics functional materials materials design and engineering nanomagnetism and multiferroics
optics and photonics theoretical and experimental research in nano photonics light in disordered media ultrafast optics nonlinear optics topological singularities specially optical fibers photonic sensors time dynamic systems terahertz generation and application transient and steady state spectroscopy bound state in the continuum disorder photons and light matter interaction and ceramic materials for high energy laser applications
candidates interested in pursuing ph please go through the research groups and visit the individual faculty home pages
the application can be submitted with this link
infrastructure the department is equipped with high end research equipment which includes squid magnetometer physical property measurement systems ppms raman spectrometer and scanning tunnelling microscope stm besides host of other advanced characterization equipment which is available at the centre for research development of scientific instruments crdsi
collaborations the department is having close collaboration with number of national and international agencies including drdo isro dae csir dst and serb
department faculties also have collaborations with various institutes of national and international repute namely tifr iit bombay iit gandhinagar iit bhu central university of rajasthan university of montreal canada university of warsaw poland brookhaven national laboratory usa eth zurich switzerland imem parma italy infn turino italy cmu pittsburgh usa belle ii kek japan etc

they published their work in the journ jitendra yadav has received the galileo galilei poster award at the international symposium on beyond li ion batteries held in padova italy sept
ph students sipra mohapatra and hema teherpuria have been awarded the prestigious aps travel grant and best poster award for hema at ccp2024 our phd student miss surbhi ramawat has received the best poster award with cash prize of euro in one of the globally renowned conference on thermoelectric materials

office of administration iit jodhpur
welcome to office of administration the office of administration at iit jodhpur ensures the efficient functioning of the institute administrative ecosystem through its various divisions activities directly or with the help of professor in charges pic given as follows establishment non faculty managing service matters related to non teaching staff
non faculty recruitment handling end to end recruitment processes for non teaching positions
health center ensuring quality healthcare services for the campus community
campus security and transport overseeing campus safety and transport logistics
logistics and facilities managing day to day campus services infrastructure maintenance and operational support
green cell driving sustainability initiatives and environmental stewardship on campus
visitor hostels and allied services coordinating hospitality services for guests visitors and campus events
internal audit ensuring internal controls compliance and financial accountability
store and purchase managing procurement processes and inventory control for efficient resource utilization
administrative manpower hiring for the project
the office functions with the aim of ensuring transparency in the process efficiency accountability and excellence
working closely with different offices sections the office aims to deliver high quality and timely services
it also frames and implements policies to foster responsive culture to support institutional goals in education development and the well being of the campus community
dean of administration prof shree prakash tiwari email phone prof shree prakash tiwari assumed the office as the first dean of administration doad of iit jodhpur on 15th june
doad position was created in as part of administrative reforms for efficient functioning
prof tiwari is currently professor in department of electrical engineering
prior to joining iit jodhpur as faculty member in prof tiwari had worked as postdoctoral fellow in school of ece at georgia institute of technology atlanta usa for years
he had done his ph from ee department iit bombay from to during which he had also worked at ntu singapore for about months
prof tiwari is senior member of ieee and serves as selected member of technical committees on flexible electronics and displays and electronic materials of ieee electron devices society
prof tiwari was visvesvaraya young faculty research fellow of ministry of electronics and information technology government of india for
he was part of indian delegate to attend indo german frontiers of engineering symposium in bremen germany organized by alexander von humboldt foundation and department of science and technology followed by connect follow up programme award by humboldt foundation and igstc connect plus award to visit and work in germany in
at iit jodhpur he leads the flexible large area microelectronics flame research group with focus towards biodegradable electronics
he was recently conferred with institute award for research excellence senior researcher award engineering from iit jodhpur
he has co authored over research articles including more than in journals of high repute with many in ieee acs and wiley journals

the department is looking forward to working on problems that throw up intellectual challenges and demand practical and impactful applications and solutions
besides this technology development initiatives and exploration of an idea related to process and design improvement are also of considerable interest
the department seeks partnerships that recognise and capitalize on relative strengths of academia and the industry
to achieve these goals the department is focusing on development of strong industry collaborations in research and specialized human resource development

laboratories physics iit jodhpur
laboratories the department of physics puts strong emphasis on experiential learning aspects as an integral part of its curriculum for msc as well as ph students
this allows the students to learn theoretical concepts much quicker and better through hands on experience
the department thus offers several laboratory courses for msc student as prescribed in the program structure
further the lab experiments are designed in such way that the students can comprehend the theoretical concepts taught in the class room and develop the skill sets which will enable them excel in their future professional endeavours
there has been tremendous growth in computational capabilities in past few decades
this has brought significant changes in the approaches towards research not only in engineering domains but also the areas of core sciences
the use of advanced computational tools has made it possible to perform experiments on virtual platforms through the process of simulation and modeling
this has significantly improved the pace at which the research outputs are obtained
today many of the outputs of simulation and modeling are applied directly in design and development of systems and technologies
hence it has been endeavour of the department to ensure that the computational laboratories are given due emphasis along with the laboratories with advanced equipment
keeping this in view the department has set up number of laboratories for teaching and research
the teaching labs of the department are equipped with some specialized instruments which the students can also use for their research activities as part of their project and thesis
in order to facilitate globally competitive cutting edge research and breakthrough technologies it is imperative to develop an atmosphere wherein the students and faculty members have free access to research facilities not only within the department but across all the departments oft he institute
with this philosophy in mind the department is operating various thematic research labs wherein any student or faculty member can carry out his her research
the department has set up four focussed research groups which carry out fundamental and applied research in the areas of quantum physics ii high energy and astrophysics iii optics and photonics and iv condensed matter and plasma physics
these research groups are supported by the thematic research laboratories catering to the need of the faculty members phd students and research staffs
the details of laboratories teaching research lab thematic research lab and research groups of the department are given below
the laboratories of the department are managed under the supervision of faculty members of the department with the support of qualified technical staff and ph students

computer science ii selected topics in computer science iii special topics in algorithms level courses compulsory courses cse and ai ds course title data structures and algorithms course no
csxxx department computer science and engineering structure offered for tech of all branches type compulsory prerequisite introduction to computer programming antirequisite none objectives
to introduce and practice the implementation of various data structures used for indexing searching and sorting operations
to introduce basic mathematical techniques for algorithm analysis and design
ability to design and implement appropriate data structures for indexing searching and sorting operations for real world problems
designing of new algorithms using standard data structures
analyzing the time and space complexities of standard data structures and basic algorithms
contents algorithm analysis and complexity big little oh omega theta notation recurrence equations
lectures abstract data types linear data structures tree binary trees tree traversal applications
lectures search trees binary search trees balanced search trees avl trees trees
lectures heaps binary heap heap order property and min max heaps
lectures sets disjoint set adt basic operations on sets union find algorithm
lectures sorting algorithms bubble sort selection sort bucket sort insertion sort overview of divide and conquer quick sort merge sort
lectures hashing hash tables and operations hash function open and closed hashing external and internal hashing collision resolving methods rehashing
lectures graph algorithms de nitions branch and bound backtracking representation traversal shortest path algorithms minimum spanning tree algorithm topological sorting
lectures greedy techniques and dynamic programming lectures laboratory
implementation of data structures using programming language
practically verifying and comparing run time performance and asymptotic behavior of various data structures and related algorithms
applications of data structures from real life scenarios
text book weiss data structures and algorithm analysis in addison wesley 2nd edition
reference book cormen leiserson
rivest stein introduction to algorithms mit press 3rd edition

knapsack lectures linear programming canonical and standard forms feasibility and optimization simplex algorithm

level elective course title vehicular ad hoc networks vanets number csl7xx0 department computer science and engineering offered for
type elective prerequisite computer networks introduction to wireless ad hoc networks antirequisite none objectives the instructor will
introduce the emerging technologies standards and applications in vehicular communication systems
provide the design considerations and challenges of vehicle to infrastructure and vehicle to vehicle communications
theories such as vehicular mobility modeling and vehicular technologies and standards from the physical to network layers will be introduced in the course
examples of emerging applications of vehicular communications in intelligent transportation systems will also be studied and discussed
learning outcomes the students are expected to have the ability to
understand and describe the basic theories and principles technologies standards and system architecture of vehicular ad hoc networks vanet or inter vehicle communication networks
analyze design and evaluate vehicular communication platforms for various kinds of safety and infotainment applications

chemmeet chemistry iit jodhpur
chemmeet the department of chemistry organized its first in house symposium chemmeet on 5th october
the program started at am in the morning and continued up to pm in the evening
the aim of this one day symposium was to bring together all the departmental faculties and students and make each stakeholder acquainted with the cutting edge fundamental and interdisciplinary research programs ongoing in the department
this event comprised invited lectures by departmental faculty members and ph students
in addition there was poster session by the students

mt options for specializations minors and dual degree back to index major department specialization minor interdisciplinary specialization back to index eligibility criteria back to index student should have earned minimum of graded credits with the cgpa more than or equal to at the end of the 5th semester to opt for capability linked opportunity student who has completed graded credits can take level courses
credits requirements back to index student needs to complete total of credits in the major department specialization or minor interdisciplinary specialization area offered ten credits out of will be taken up as part of open electives where as the student is required to opt for an additional credits in the form of course overload this will be indicated in the degree starting from the sixth semester students who have earned graded credits with cgpa more than or equal to are permitted to take up to extra credits as part of their normal load which forms part of her his additional learning component every semester in addition to the prescribed courses for their degree table ugprogramoptions use the horizontal scroll bar to see the full table institute core program linked program core open specialization minor tech mba total graded non graded total graded non graded program engineering ie science is humanities ih science ls program compulsory pc program electives pe tech

credits requirements for tech tech mba dual degree in the chosen specialisation back to index student needs to complete total of graded and non graded credits in the minor department or interdisciplinary area offered towards tech and graded and non graded credits towards mba it will include credits earned for major department or or minor interdisciplinary area specialization excluding the project credits credits of minor specialization done in semester vi and semester vii if not part of tech mba programme will be counted towards programme electives students will be required to complete all core courses except for preparatory and bridge courses students will be required to take additional credit of programme elective against preparatory courses

faculty computer science engineering iit jodhpur
faculty welcome to faculty position the department of computer science and engineering at iit jodhpur invites applications for faculty positions at all levels
candidates should have phd degree in cse or related field
the candidates are expected to have excellent research track record and commitment towards teaching
while we encourage candidates from all areas of cse to apply special emphasis will be given to the following areas algorithms quantum computation information security database systems data engineering cloud technologies edge and fog computing computer graphics animation augmented and virtual reality data visualisation computer vision human machine interface information retrieval artificial intelligence and machine learning natural language understanding software systems and engineering programming languages and compilers social and complex networks autonomous systems high performance computing parallel programming gpu programming distributed systems os and networking 5g and 5g systems sensors and iot
about iit jodhpur iitj has sprawling campus of over acres with students and faculty members with the department of cse having faculty members
the department coordinates the btech mtech and mtech phd programs in cse and ai along with phd in cse
more details about the department can be found at
the institute also houses the technology and innovation hub on cv ar and vr
any indian national pio or foreign national is eligible to apply
the rules of government of india will be applicable for terms and conditions of the appointments
we offer salary and benefits as per the norms of the central government
benefits include relocation expenses maternity paternity leaves generous initiation grant phd student support on campus housing if available access to on campus medical facilities access to on campus sports facilities etc
in case of any clarification candidates may reach out to
interested candidates are encouraged to apply via
why choose iit jodhpur opportunity to work with diverse group of very talented faculty members in diverse areas of computer science
the department and the institute highly also encourage inter departmental collaboration
the department has high end computational facilities available in addition to the central facilities available with the institute
some of the departmental facilities include dgx multiple servers vanet lab and ar vr lab
the dgx2 server is high performance computing system designed specifically for artificial intelligence ai and deep learning applications
this server is equipped with nvidia tesla v100 gpus which provide massive parallel processing power and high bandwidth memory to support large scale machine learning models
it comes with nvlink technology which enables fast inter connectivity between gpus as well as high speed nvme storage system for efficient data processing
with these capabilities the dgx2 server can handle demanding ai workloads with ease making it an ideal resource for researchers and developers in the department
the department also has several high end workstations servers with nvidia a5000 and nvidia 1080ti designed for individual use
these workstations are equipped with powerful cpus high speed storage and advanced gpus making them suitable for range of computational tasks including data analysis machine learning and scientific computing
the department has several servers that are used for various purposes including web hosting database management and file storage
these servers are equipped with powerful cpus and storage making them suitable for handling large amounts of data and serving multiple users simultaneously
generous seed grant of rs
lakhs for new faculty members to kick off their research
engage in the department growth by being part of designing the policies and course curriculum
regular talks by leading experts in various disciplines
location at the picturesque jodhpur campus close to mother nature
freedom to organise events and activities for the advancement of your research in the community
enthusiastic departmental staff members to handle day to day requests
eligibility scale of pay selection procedure apply online important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur

transportation engineering civil and infrastructure engineering iit jodhpur
transportation engineering transportation engineering lab is well equipped with the basic as well as advance equipments such as marshall stability test apparatus rotational viscoemeter cannon manning viscoemeter asphalt mix theoretical density meter humidity chamber rolling thin film oven automatic marshall compactor etc
research groups in this domain focus on the topics such as pavement analysis pavement design sustainable pavement materials traffic flow theory macroscopic and microscopic modelling of traffic flow connected and autonomous vehicles dynamic traffic assignment intelligent transportation system etc
wheel rut tracker wheel rut tracker the matest smartracker multi wheel hamburg wheel tracker is high performance testing system used to assess the rutting and moisture susceptibility of asphalt mixtures under controlled conditions
compliant with en and aashto t324 standards it features dual independent wheels for simultaneous wet and dry testing precise temperature regulation and automated data acquisition
its advanced design ensures accurate measurement of rut depth creep slope and stripping inflection points making it an essential tool for evaluating long term pavement performance in laboratory settings

all news indian institute of technology jodhpur iit jodhpur
all news congratulations to dr sandip murarka for receiving the crsi bronze medal dr sandip murarka has been conferred the prestigious crsi bronze medal by the chemical research society of india crsi at the recently concluded crsi national symposium in chemistry held at dibrugarh university
this distinguished recognition acknowledges his significant contributions to the fields of organic synthesis and catalysis
iit jodhpur congratulates dr murarka on this well deserved honor and wishes him continued success in his future endeavors

collaborations electrical engineering iit jodhpur
collaborations faculty members of the department of electrical engineering are keen to collaborate with individuals from academia laboratories and industry in india and abroad in areas of mutual interest
some of the current collaborations are as follows research group at iit jodhpur is developing compact models for spice circuit simulation in collaboration with uc berkeley
with carleton university joint research on signal integrity and power integrity is going on actively
further one of our faculty members is an adjunct faculty member at carleton university
with tima laboratory collaboration is on design of high speed ics and on chip noise jitter modeling
this collaboration focuses on cooperative control of passivity short systems
passivity is defined as an input output behavior of dynamical system and closely related to the principle of energy conservation
in practice however most of the systems are not well behaved and possess shortage of passivity or non passiveness in their operation
the scope of this collaboration deals with the analysis and synthesis of the networks comprising the passive short systems
with ntnu iit jodhpur is actively involved in research related to mitigating the challenges in physical layer security of communication systems and improving the performance of free space optical communication systems
this collaboration focuses on macromodeling and eda algorithm development for vlsi systems
phd student is being jointly supervised by faculty members from both the institutes
this collaboration focuses on secure coordination and control of multi agent systems
the collaboration is part of the nm icps technology innovation hub tih in robotics and autonomous systems proposed by iisc bangalore
the scope of collaboration includes designing distributed guidance and control strategies for the multivehicle network systems for their secure and robust coordination and control against malicious adversaries
there are collaborations among multiple groups of iit jodhpur and iit delhi
these collaborations involve the performance analysis of 5g and beyond communication systems and exploring the ways of improving physical layer security ii data processing and machine learning for the sensors data iii detection of breast cancer through optical techniques iv implementation of mmwave antennas for 5g test bed user equipment research group at iit jodhpur is working in collaboration with iit kanpur for the characterization and modeling of advanced semiconductor devices measurement of microwave and mmwave antennas and circuits
with iit mandi research on high speed system design and noise modeling in vlsi systems is going on
there is phd student jointly supervised by faculty members from both the institutes
the collaboration with iiit mk is on neuromorphic circuits and systems
iitj is collaborating with these institutions for the design and development of an indigenous dual frequency navic receiver
iitj work includes design and development of baseband signal processing algorithms for correlator receivers that perform signal acquisition code and carrier tracking control lock detection accumulated delta range computation and data demodulation
these algorithms would be translated to digital asic based correlator hardware by iit bombay iit madras iist thiruvananthapuram sameer
isro is providing required technological know how for various aspects related to system engineering for navic receivers

the scope of collaboration includes designing distributed guidance and control strategies for the multivehicle network systems for their secure and robust coordination and control against malicious adversaries
there are collaborations among multiple groups of iit jodhpur and iit delhi
these collaborations involve the performance analysis of 5g and beyond communication systems and exploring the ways of improving physical layer security ii data processing and machine learning for the sensors data iii detection of breast cancer through optical techniques iv implementation of mmwave antennas for 5g test bed user equipment research group at iit jodhpur is working in collaboration with iit kanpur for the characterization and modeling of advanced semiconductor devices measurement of microwave and mmwave antennas and circuits
with iit mandi research on high speed system design and noise modeling in vlsi systems is going on
there is phd student jointly supervised by faculty members from both the institutes
the collaboration with iiit mk is on neuromorphic circuits and systems
iitj is collaborating with these institutions for the design and development of an indigenous dual frequency navic receiver
iitj work includes design and development of baseband signal processing algorithms for correlator receivers that perform signal acquisition code and carrier tracking control lock detection accumulated delta range computation and data demodulation
these algorithms would be translated to digital asic based correlator hardware by iit bombay iit madras iist thiruvananthapuram sameer
isro is providing required technological know how for various aspects related to system engineering for navic receivers

research highlights chemistry iit jodhpur
research highlights agarwala pal hazra sasmal differential mg2 deposition on dna holliday junctions dictates the rate and stability of conformational exchange just accepted in nanoscale dna holliday junctions hjs are crucial intermediates in genetic recombination and genome repair processes characterized by dynamic nature and transitioning among multiple conformations on the timescale ranging from sub milliseconds to seconds
although the influence of ions on hj dynamics has been extensively studied precise quantification of the thermodynamic feasibility of transitions and detailed kinetic cooperativity remain unexplored
understanding the heterogeneity of stochastic gene recombination using ensemble averaged experimental techniques is extremely difficult because of its lack of ability to differentiate dynamics and function in high spatiotemporal resolution
herein we developed new technique that combines single molecule fluorescence resonance energy transfer smfret experiments and molecular simulation to investigate the kinetic choreography and preferential stability of hj conformations under ionic conditions that closely mimic the physiological environment relevant to cellular biology
our findings predict the prevalence of three distinct conformational macrostates in hj dynamics
at low ion concentrations hjs transition rapidly among three thermodynamically stable conformational macrostates
however in physiological ionic environment the open conformation becomes predominant
using kinetic network model based on the multi order time correlation function tcf we delineated thermodynamic parameters that govern heterogeneous dynamics as function of divalent ion concentration
stabilization of conformations due to an ionic environment and activation barriers concertedly affect transition rates between open and closed conformations
furthermore we observed significant enhancement of mg2 condensation in the central region of hjs rather than branch ends leading to plausible conclusion that the differential stability of conformational states may be governed by the junction region of hjs rather than duplex branches
this study gives new insight into the complex interplay between the ionic environment and hj dynamics offering comprehensive understanding of their behavior under conditions relevant to cellular biology and roles in key biological processes for creating heterogeneous nature of life

research highlights electrical engineering iit jodhpur
research highlights dr akshay moudgil awarded teaching excellence award teaching excellence award citation indian institute of technology jodhpur proudly bestows this award upon dr akshay assistant professor department of electrical engineering for his exceptional contributions to teaching and the overall development of students and alumni
dr akshay passion for teaching shines from the very first class where he makes complex topics accessible through interactive discussions and hands on learning experiences
beyond academics he is constant source of motivation offering patient and thoughtful guidance during research challenges and career uncertainties
always available beyond regular hours he supports students with dedication and care
his commitment to teaching and research enthusiasm problem solving approach and continuous pursuit of knowledge have inspired students to embrace perseverance curiosity and personal growth
iit jodhpur extends its heartfelt congratulations to dr akshay and wishes him continued success in his professional journey

digital infrastructure automation dia iit jodhpur
welcome to the digital infrastructure automation computer center the digital infrastructure automation dia commonly known as computer center of the indian institute of technology jodhpur stands as testament to the institution commitment to cutting edge research innovation and technological development
as pivotal hub for computing and information technology the computer center has played crucial role in shaping the academic landscape and the advancement of the institute
since its inception the computer center has undergone remarkable development and growth aligning itself with advancements in the field of computing
equipped with state of the art infrastructure and team of highly skilled professionals the center has fostered an environment conducive to research and learning
read more quick access erp portal private cloud moodle lab booking system dia verticals computer center network cybersecurity erp automation objective the center primary objective is to provide computational resources technical support and expertise to students faculty members and researchers across various disciplines
its advanced computing facilities include high performance computing clusters ai supercomputers and specialized software
these resources empower the iit jodhpur community to undertake complex computational tasks simulate intricate models and conduct data intensive research

designs desert ecosystem innovations guided by natural selection
31st july am on behalf of the department of bioscience and bioengineering the organizing team of designs desert ecosystem innovations guided by natural selection invites you to join us for jamboree on the 31st of july
this event is organised by the departm ent of bioscience and bioengineering in collaboration with the jodhpur city knowledge and innovation cluster
the jamboree will provide platform to present and discuss different aspects and perspectives on desert sciences around the world including the har that provide interesting insights on evolution and adaptation of different life forms in arid regions through biological innovations
this day long event would have convergence of themes from diverse disciplines geology genomics desert medicine from modern and traditional knowledge systems bioengineering nano medicine digital health technologies engineering and social sciences
esides members of the jckic cluster we would also have special invited lecture by dr pranay lal the author of indica that traces the natural history of the indian subcontinent through evidences from different streams
he is biochemist and an artis who works in public health and environment
also in the panel along with other leaders we would also have mr pradip krishen who besides being renowned filmmaker is now famously called as ecological gardener and has been the chief architect of the rewi lding scheme at rao jodha desert rock park near mehrangarh fort in jodhpur rajasthan
there would also be panel discussion to deliberate on challenges and opportunities in the thar ecosystem region and how we could develop transdisciplinary approach for understanding metasystems of thar
this could yield novel biological insights into daptive mechanisms and evolution enable designs of innovative bioinspired engineering solutions provide sustainable solutions for conserved ecosystem and aid in understanding human health and development of disease in arid regions
we would have posters by student groups and flash presentations of the best five posters
we have also planned demonstration of an app by the student community group of iit for citizen science approach to understanding flora and fauna of thar
the event would be held online and we would share further event details soon
we look forward to your active participation

winter school iit jodhpur
welcome to the winter school on ai and tcs the department of computer science and engineering and tih ihub drishti are jointly organizing the third winter school on artificial intelligence and theoretical computer science
the school is being organized as an in person event
this year the school will be focused on generative ai december computation in social choice and economics december students who successfully complete the winter school and obtain participation certificate will be prioritized for summer winter internship at the department of computer science and engineering iit jodhpur under the mentorship of dr pallavi jain or dr pratik mazumder and or dr anand mishra
important dates school december deadline of registration november we also have poster session on december focusing on computation in social choice and economics

research highlights physics iit jodhpur
research highlights dr yogeshwar nath mishra has co invented the world fastest single shot wide field microscope with his collaborators at nasa caltech in usa and fau in germany
they published their work in the journal nature communications
congratulations dr yogeshwar nath mishra and his collaborators in usa and germany for publishing their joint collaborative work entitled single shot two dimensional nano size mapping of fluorescent molecules by ultrafast polarization anisotropy imaging in nature communications
it is proud moment for the department
the team has developed compressed ultrafast planar polarization anisotropy imaging cup2ai which they describe as the world fastest wide field microscopic imaging tool for real time sizing of fluorescent molecules in liquids and gases in 2d at hundreds of billions of frames per second
it was tested on fluorescein dextran commonly known as fitc dextran conjugate of fluorescein and dextran natural glucose based polymer used to mimic biomolecules
fluorescein is dye also found in food and drink coloring
fitc is biodegradable water soluble and widely used in applications such as monitoring blood flow drug delivery and tissue and cell labeling
additionally cup2ai was employed to map polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons pahs in flames which are precursors to black carbon nanoparticles substances known to pose significant environmental and health risks
while traditional techniques often rely on time consuming point by point scanning cup2ai captures the scene in single shot in 2d delivering real time data without the need for temporal averaging or spatial scanning
it achieves imaging speeds of hundreds of billions of frames per second enabling the observation of molecular interactions and delicate samples in unprecedented slow motion
this technology holds significant potential for applications in disease detection biomarker development and pharmaceutical research
it can also be applied to study nanoparticle growth and aggregation particle sizing in combustion processes and the spray drying industry
the published paper can be accessed from here important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur

faculty members bioscience bioengineering iit jodhpur
faculty members applications are invited from indian and foreign nationals with exceptional academic record relevant teaching experience excellent research record and valuable industrial experience for contractual and full time positions at various academic levels professor associate professor and assistant professor in the department of bioscience and bioengineering
new faculty members are expected to carry out teaching learning and research in the following sub disciplines of focus areas of the department to develop technological solutions for healthcare the environment and agriculture

the department also welcomes applicants with background in other relevant fields of bio science and bio engineering
applicants should preferably have an undergraduate degree in an engineering discipline relevant to above fields from reputed institute or university along with good academic record throughout from class
the institute is building extensive state of the art centralized laboratory facilities with many advanced equipment to undertake research and development
further the department also encourages faculty members to interact with the industry and national laboratories to conduct collaborative research and development in nationally relevant areas
the department offers masters and doctoral degree programs in bioscience bioengineering and will also offer undergraduate program in biotechnology

latest news mathematics iit jodhpur
latest news workshop on lean the department of mathematics is organizing an introductory workshop on the lean proof assistant from 26th to 28th february
the workshop will introduce the participants to theorem proving in lean using the mathlib library and building definitions in lean
no prior experience with lean is needed all faculty phd scholars and masters students in the department are welcome
the workshop will be led by dr prathamesh assistant professor of computer science krea university
to get feel for theorem proving in lean we will organize mini seminars from 23rd to 25th february where we will play the natural number game and get started with installing lean
please fill in this google form to indicate your interest in participating in the workshop 2mhdghzwchyueqxn7 below is brief outline of the topics that will be covered during the workshop

talk title introducing lean what it is why it matters and how it works
duration hour tutorial title introduction to proving in lean duration hours

talk title navigating lean mathlib library projects in lean and other documentation
duration mins towards the later half or the end of the session
friday afternoon 27th feb in lhc ii from pm to pm tutorial title proving in lean intermediate tactics duration hours
talk title demystifying lean type theory primer for tactics and blackboxes
saturday morning 28th feb in lhc ii from pm to pm tutorial title definitions structures and programs in lean duration hours

postgraduate program bioscience bioengineering iit jodhpur
postgraduate program introduction the biological world is full of natural wonders beginning from microscopic bacteria to multicellular organisms
using reductionist approach each organism can be broken down into its subsystems which continue to provide us with new knowledge on how evolution has perfected these systems over millennia
conversely one can also take systems biology approach and appreciate the whole organism as system that grows and continuously adapts to the environment
beginning from thermodynamically efficient cellular systems to the molecular motors of the flagellar motor each product of evolution is an optimized engineering design that can be translated into real life applications across disciplines
furthermore the call for support from today data driven biology has been answered by advancements in computational biology that permit solving biology problems using artificial intelligence and machine learning approaches
genomics proteomics transcriptomics and metabolomics have transformed the landscape of infectious and non infectious diseases allowing the development of new diagnostic measures as well as therapeutics
in combination with active contribution from electronic mechanical materials engineering implantable sensor and support devices that are biocompatible have significantly contributed to extending the healthy lifespan of the human race
the department of bioscience bioengineering aspires to cater to the technological requirements of the country by conducting high quality translatable and interdisciplinary research in the aforementioned focus areas
objectives of the program to produce professionals with an in depth knowledge of biological systems and phenomena with critical thinking and bioengineering research skills that can be effectively put to use in solving problems in focus domains including healthcare energy environment and agriculture
the program will help graduates to approach problems in the above focus domains by providing knowledge and hands on laboratory training in the areas of data driven multi omics technologies artificial intelligence computational biology systems biology imaging based diagnostics biomaterials and bio therapeutics
expected graduate attributes graduates will be able to think critically and solve problems that are necessary for employment in biomedical biotechnology pharmaceutical industries national research labs and academic institutions at the global level
develop sense of ownership on the national missions for technology development which will cater to societal needs such as affordable healthcare clean energy safe environment and sustainable agriculture
be exceptionally well trained in the laboratory enabling them to conduct high quality reproducible research
be able to read understand and review intellectual property documents and recognize their rights
be able to express their research ideas and present their data clearly in written and spoken format
develop strong work ethic and understand ethical considerations in biological research which are both necessary for high quality research
develop healthy time management skills to plan and manage laboratory work as well as other miscellaneous research associated demands on their time to effectively meet deadlines
learning outcomes graduates will be introduced to cutting edge interdisciplinary research areas like diagnostics therapeutics development instrumentation design development multi omics technologies applications biomedical imaging image processing systems synthetic biology and biomaterials engineering artificial intelligence for biological applications be exposed to common problems that are prevalent in the domains of affordable healthcare clean energy safe environment and sustainable agriculture allowing them to innovate and develop technological solutions for the same
receive effective hands on laboratory training as part of laboratory courses and short term research projects that are designed to teach research methodology and common lab techniques
be introduced to patents and other intellectual property documents the rights that they grant and necessary considerations while filing patents technology that is protected by patents
develop their research communication skills by participating in seminars journal clubs manuscript writing and research grant writing workshops
receive training on building work ethic by being part of research groups working towards common goal
graduates will also appreciate the ethical and safety considerations while conducting biomedical research
be taught how to prioritize items on their schedule and how to budget time across the spectrum of activities including research and other necessary tasks
topic clouds for the list of topic clouds and mapping of the topic with the courses download file kb

research highlights physics iit jodhpur
research highlights ph students sipra mohapatra and hema teherpuria have been awarded the prestigious aps travel grant and best poster award for hema at ccp2024 our ph students sipra mohapatra and hema teherpuria have been awarded the prestigious aps travel grant to present their research work at the xxxv iupap conference on computational physics ccp2024 from july hosted at aristotle university in thessaloniki greece
sipra delivered an oral presentation at the conference where her work received excellent appreciation and valuable suggestions for future advancements from leading experts in the field
additionally hema co advised by dr prabhat jaiswal was honored with the best poster award at ccp2024
both sipra and hema have demonstrated exceptional dedication in unraveling fundamental ion transport mechanisms in battery electrolytes
these achievements reflect their hard work and the support they have received from the physics community at iit jodhpur
details of the research presented the impact of pectin on transport and mechanical properties of ec litfsi electrolytes sipra mohapatra salt effects on ionic conductivity mechanisms in ethylene carbonate electrolytes interplay of viscosity and ion ion relaxations hema teherpuria important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee intranet links copyright all rights reserved this portal is owned designed and developed by digital infrastructure automation iit jodhpur
